domain: Failure-mode & robustness analysis of PRISM (local, backprop-free predictive-coding cortical workspace network for neuromorphic hardware)
summary: I analyze how PRISM fails on each open problem P1-P6 and give a local, weight-transport-free mitigation plus a measurable diagnostic for each. The decisive finding is that the "single gate for attention AND plasticity" hypothesis is INTERNALLY CONTRADICTORY at the sign level and cannot stand as one scalar: PC's native rule makes a confident channel learn FASTER (dw ~ Pi*eps*r), which is exactly backwards for consolidation. The fix is to keep ONE gate variable g_i (the precision-weighted-error bid) but route it through DIFFERENT functional transforms for the two roles: inference gain is monotone-increasing in instantaneous bid, while the plasticity rate is gated by a SLOW, separately-tracked metaplastic variable theta_i that integrates how long the bid has been low (mastery), so attention rises with surprise while plasticity falls with sustained low surprise. This is two coupled variables sharing one driver, not one variable — I argue the brief's single-gate claim is only defensible in this decoupled-timescale form, and say so honestly. I then give: a metaplastic consolidation term with an anti-rigidity floor (P3), an inter-module agreement/consensus penalty that makes greedy routing globally consistent without weight transport (P4), an explicit load-balancing entropy term with its closed-form local gradient and a homeostatic threshold-adaptation rule (P6), and a divisive-normalization + leaky-integrator damping that provably bounds the rich-get-richer fixed point. Every mitigation uses only locally available signals (own error, own activity, broadcast scalar, lateral gate sums) and never reads W^T. P1 and P2 are flagged as genuinely unsolved; I give diagnostics that would falsify optimistic assumptions rather than claiming a solution.

equations.name: Core gate decoupling (resolves the sign contradiction)
equations.math:
bid_i = pi_i * ||eps_i||^2 ;  g_i = softmax_or_topk(bid_i)  [routing+attention gain, FAST, increasing in surprise]
 theta_i(t+1) = (1-rho)*theta_i(t) + rho*(1 - g_i(t))   [metaplastic mastery trace, SLOW, rho<<1]
 eta_i = eta0 * g_i * (1 - theta_i) + eta_floor   [effective plasticity rate]
 dw_i ~ NM * eta_i * eps_i * r_i * e_i   [three/four-factor Hebbian]
equations.explanation: ONE driver (the bid) feeds TWO transforms. Inference gain g_i increases with instantaneous surprise (attention). Plasticity rate eta_i is suppressed by the slow mastery trace theta_i: a module that has won-then-stopped-winning (low g_i sustained) accumulates theta_i->1 and freezes. This makes attention and consolidation share a gate WITHOUT the sign contradiction, because the consolidation signal is the time-integral of LOW bid, not the bid itself. Honest caveat: this is two coupled state variables (g_i fast, theta_i slow), not literally one scalar; the brief's single-gate claim survives only in this decoupled-timescale reading. eta_floor > 0 is the anti-rigidity guarantee (see P3).

equations.name: P3 anti-rigidity floor + reawakening (stability-plasticity)
equations.math:
eta_i = eta_floor + (eta0 - eta_floor) * g_i * (1 - theta_i)
 theta_i(t+1) = (1-rho)*theta_i + rho*(1 - g_i) - kappa * relu(bid_i - tau_wake)
  with eta_floor>0,  kappa>0 (surprise actively DE-consolidates)
equations.explanation: Pure consolidation (theta->1, eta->0) is 'rigidity death': the module can never relearn if the world changes. Two local fixes: (1) eta_floor>0 keeps a permanent minimum plasticity so a frozen module still tracks slow drift; (2) the -kappa*relu(bid - tau_wake) term lets a LARGE prediction error actively decrement theta_i, reopening the learning window on genuine novelty (metaplastic reawakening). All terms use only module-local bid_i and the module's own theta_i.

equations.name: P4 global-coherence consensus penalty (no weight transport)
equations.math:
F_total = sum_i pi_i*||eps_i||^2  +  lambda * sum_{(i,j) in N} ||A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j||^2
 activity update: dz_i/dt = -pi_i * (d eps_i/d z_i) - 2*lambda * A_ij^T (A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j) + noise
 A_ij learned locally by dA_ij ~ -(A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j) z_i^T
equations.explanation: Greedy per-module routing can settle into locally-optimal but globally-inconsistent latent assignments (each module 'explains' its slice with mutually contradictory causes). A lateral consistency term penalizes disagreement on the shared overlap (A_ij projects module i's latent into the shared subspace with neighbor j). This is the workspace's global energy made explicit as pairwise lateral predictions. Crucially A_ij and A_ji are SEPARATE learned matrices (no W^T): the symmetry that backprop would assume is replaced by learned approximate symmetry, paying an accuracy cost (relates to P2).

equations.name: P6 load-balancing entropy term + its local gradient
equations.math:
F_load = -beta * H(p)  where p_i = <g_i>_batch (running mean win-share),  H(p) = -sum_i p_i log p_i
 dF_load/d(score_i) = beta * p_i * ( log p_i - sum_k p_k log p_k )  [via softmax Jacobian]
 => bias update:  b_i <- b_i - mu * beta * (log p_i - H_term),  i.e. raise threshold of over-used experts
 homeostatic form:  tau_i(t+1) = tau_i(t) + mu*(p_i - 1/M)   [target uniform win-share 1/M over M experts]
equations.explanation: Without pressure, a few experts capture all bids and the rest receive zero gradient and die (P6). The negative-entropy term rewards spreading wins across experts; its gradient through the softmax raises the effective threshold of frequently-winning modules. The homeostatic threshold form is even simpler and fully local: each module nudges its OWN bid threshold tau_i up when its running win-share p_i exceeds the fair share 1/M. Only a running scalar p_i (the module's own win frequency) and the constant 1/M are needed.

equations.name: PRISM-specific rich-get-richer damping
equations.math:
score_i = bid_i / (sigma + sum_j bid_j)   [divisive normalization across modules]
 win_i(t) = (1-alpha)*win_i(t-1) + alpha*1[i wins]   [leaky integrator]
 effective_bid_i = bid_i * (1 - gamma*win_i)   [self-inhibition / fatigue]
 stable fixed point requires gamma > d(bid_i)/d(win_i) at the attractor
equations.explanation: Bid-based routing has a positive feedback trap: winning -> learns -> lower error elsewhere is NOT guaranteed; a winner can keep refining its own niche, raising its own reliability pi_i, raising its bid, winning more (rich-get-richer), starving others. Three stacked dampers: (1) divisive normalization makes bids competitive so no module's absolute scale runs away; (2) a leaky win-integrator win_i; (3) a fatigue term that subtracts gamma*win_i from the effective bid, so a chronic winner is progressively self-inhibited. The inequality gamma > d(bid)/d(win) is the local stability condition that prevents a single-winner attractor.

equations.name: P5 Langevin settling vs P3 noise-floor interaction
equations.math:
dz_i = -nabla_{z_i} F dt + sqrt(2 T_i) dW_i ,  T_i = T0 * g_i
 consolidation must use plasticity noise floor:  Var(dw_i) bounded => need eta_i*||eps_i|| small at convergence
equations.explanation: Stochastic (Langevin) settling samples the posterior (good: exploration, uncertainty). But injected noise T_i creates a residual ||eps_i||>0 even for a mastered module, which through dw ~ eta*eps*r keeps nudging weights -> a noise-driven forgetting floor. Tying temperature to the gate (T_i = T0*g_i) cools frozen modules (low g_i -> low T_i), so consolidated modules both stop learning AND stop sampling, consistent with P3. Diagnostic: measure weight drift of a mastered module under frozen input; nonzero drift reveals the noise-floor leak.

key_findings: THE CORE HYPOTHESIS IS NOT LITERALLY TRUE AS ONE SCALAR. In native PC, dw ~ Pi*eps*r couples inference weighting and learning rate with the SAME sign, so a confident/reliable channel learns FASTER — the exact opposite of consolidation. A single monotone gate cannot simultaneously implement attention (rises with surprise) and consolidation (freezes with mastery). The defensible version is ONE DRIVER (the bid) feeding TWO timescale-separated transforms: fast inference gain g_i increasing in surprise, and a slow metaplastic trace theta_i increasing in sustained mastery that suppresses plasticity. PRISM must adopt this or it is self-contradictory.

P3 (stability-plasticity) has a second-order failure beyond catastrophic forgetting: RIGIDITY DEATH. If consolidation drives eta_i->0 permanently, a mastered module can never adapt to a changed world or a covariate shift. Mitigation needs BOTH an eta_floor>0 (permanent minimum plasticity) AND an active de-consolidation term -kappa*relu(bid - tau_wake) that reopens the learning window on large surprise. Without the reawakening term, PRISM's headline 'task-boundary-free continual learning' is actually 'learn-once-then-die'.

P4 (global coherence) is the most under-defended claim. Greedy precision-weighted routing minimizes each module's LOCAL free energy but there is no guarantee the union of local minima is a global minimum — modules can settle into mutually inconsistent latent assignments that each look locally optimal. The PC global energy only couples modules through the shared workspace; that is too weak when k<<n bottleneck discards cross-module constraints. An explicit lateral consensus penalty lambda*||A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j||^2 on overlapping latents is required, with A_ij, A_ji SEPARATE learned matrices (no W^T).

P6 (dead experts) interacts with the bid mechanism to produce RICH-GET-RICHER, which is worse than passive expert death: a winner that refines its niche raises its own reliability pi_i, raises its bid, wins more, and actively STARVES competitors of gradient. Passive entropy regularization is necessary but not sufficient; you also need a fatigue/self-inhibition term effective_bid_i = bid_i*(1 - gamma*win_i) with the local stability condition gamma > d(bid)/d(win), plus divisive normalization so absolute bid scale cannot run away.

The load-balancing gradient is closed-form and fully local: with p_i = running mean of g_i and F_load = -beta*H(p), the threshold update is b_i <- b_i - mu*beta*(log p_i - sum_k p_k log p_k). Equivalently the homeostatic rule tau_i += mu*(p_i - 1/M) needs only the module's own win-share and the fair share 1/M. No global gradient transport is needed.

P5 (exploration) and P3 (consolidation) are in DIRECT tension: Langevin noise injected for posterior sampling creates a residual error floor that keeps nudging supposedly-frozen weights (noise-driven forgetting). Resolution: tie sampling temperature to the gate, T_i = T0*g_i, so consolidated (low-g) modules cool and stop both sampling and drifting. This is a previously-unstated coupling that PRISM must specify.

P1 (scaling) and P2 (backward-weight problem) are NOT solved by any mitigation here and must not be claimed as solved. P2 in particular is only RELAXED: replacing W^T with separate learned feedback weights (and the A_ij/A_ji asymmetry in P4) introduces a feedback-alignment-style approximation whose error compounds with depth — the documented reason fully-local methods have not yet matched backprop on hard tasks (P1). The honest position is that PRISM trades exact gradients for locality and pays an unknown, depth-dependent accuracy tax.
risks: Single-gate framing as literally one scalar is self-contradictory (sign clash between PC's Pi-coupling and Bayesian-synapse variance-coupling). If the team ships the one-scalar version it will EITHER never consolidate (PC sign) OR never attend (variance sign). Risk = the central novelty is mis-stated and falsifiable on day one.

Rigidity death: consolidation that lacks an eta_floor and a reawakening term yields permanent freezing; the continual-learning payoff inverts into inability to track distribution shift. High likelihood without explicit guard.

Global incoherence: greedy local routing settles into inconsistent module assignments; representation looks fine per-module but is globally nonsensical (e.g., conflicting object identities across overlapping receptive fields). The k<<n workspace bottleneck makes this more likely, not less.
Rich-get-richer collapse to a single dominant module; downstream the network behaves like one expert plus dead weight, destroying the modularity that justifies O(n*k) compute.
Noise-driven forgetting: Langevin exploration silently erodes consolidated weights through the eps residual floor unless temperature is gate-coupled. Subtle, slow, easy to miss in short experiments.

Feedback-alignment error compounding (P2): separate learned/random feedback weights misalign with the true forward Jacobian as depth grows, capping achievable performance (P1). This is the load-bearing reason backprop-parity is unproven; mitigations REDUCE but do not eliminate it.

Diagnostic blind spot: many failures (rigidity, noise-floor drift, incipient rich-get-richer) are SLOW and invisible on standard short benchmarks; without the long-horizon diagnostics below the team may declare success prematurely.

Hardware-analog mismatch: device noise, conductance drift, and finite bit-precision on crossbars can masquerade as (or amplify) the noise-floor and dead-expert failures, confounding which layer of the stack is at fault.
recommendations: Restate the core hypothesis honestly as ONE-DRIVER / TWO-TRANSFORM with explicit timescale separation: fast inference gain g_i increasing in surprise, slow metaplastic theta_i increasing in mastery and suppressing plasticity. Do not claim a single scalar. This both fixes the contradiction and is itself a defensible, sharper novelty.

Implement P3 with eta_i = eta_floor + (eta0-eta_floor)*g_i*(1-theta_i) and the de-consolidation term -kappa*relu(bid_i - tau_wake). Treat eta_floor and kappa as first-class hyperparameters; sweep them.

Add the lateral consensus energy lambda*sum_{(i,j) in N} ||A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j||^2 with separately-learned A_ij, A_ji. This is the minimal local fix for P4 and doubles as a graceful relaxation surface for the P2 weight-transport problem (let the network learn its own approximate symmetry).

Add BOTH the entropy/load term (gradient b_i -= mu*beta*(log p_i - H_term)) and the homeostatic threshold rule tau_i += mu*(p_i - 1/M) for P6; they are cheap and local. Add the fatigue damper effective_bid_i = bid_i*(1 - gamma*win_i) plus divisive normalization for the PRISM-specific rich-get-richer loop, and verify the local stability condition gamma > d(bid)/d(win) empirically near the operating point.
Couple Langevin temperature to the gate, T_i = T0*g_i, so the P5 exploration mechanism does not undermine P3 consolidation. Make this coupling explicit in the energy/dynamics spec.

Run LONG-HORIZON diagnostics, not just short benchmarks: (a) Consolidation/rigidity probe — train module to mastery, freeze input, measure weight drift over many steps (should be ~eta_floor-bounded, nonzero but tiny; zero = rigidity, large = noise-floor leak). (b) Reawakening probe — after mastery, switch the input distribution and measure recovery time and whether theta_i actually decrements. (c) Coherence probe — measure cross-module disagreement ||A_ij z_i - A_ji z_j|| over training; rising = P4 failure. (d) Load/Gini probe — track Gini coefficient of win-shares p_i and number of effectively-dead experts (p_i < epsilon); rising Gini = rich-get-richer. (e) Alignment probe (P2) — measure angle between the learned-feedback update direction and a backprop reference on small nets; growing angle with depth quantifies the accuracy tax.

Do NOT claim P1 (scaling) or P2 (weight transport) solved. Frame PRISM as buying locality/continual-learning/hardware-fit at a quantified, depth-dependent gradient-accuracy cost, and report that cost via the alignment probe.

Build an ablation matrix that toggles each mitigation independently (eta_floor, kappa, consensus lambda, entropy beta, fatigue gamma, temperature coupling) so each failure mode's diagnostic moves when and only when its guard is removed — this is the cleanest evidence the mechanisms are doing what is claimed.
novelty_assessment: Mixed and stated honestly. (1) The one-driver/two-transform resolution of the attention-vs-consolidation sign contradiction is a genuine sharpening: it reconciles PC's dw ~ Pi*eps*r (confident->fast) with Bayesian-synapse metaplasticity (uncertain->fast) by routing one bid through a fast monotone-increasing gain and a slow mastery-integrating suppressor. I am not aware of this exact decoupling stated for a PC/global-workspace gate, though each ingredient (metaplasticity a la Fusi/Benna-Fusi; precision-weighting a la Bogacz; three-factor rules a la Fremaux-Gerstner) is established — so the novelty is the COMBINATION and the explicit sign-resolution, not the parts. (2) The de-consolidation reawakening term -kappa*relu(bid - tau_wake) as an anti-rigidity guard is a natural but, to my knowledge, not-standard addition to gated-plasticity continual learners. (3) The load-balancing entropy gradient and homeostatic threshold are STANDARD (mixture-of-experts / Switch-Transformer load balancing; the homeostatic-threshold trick is classic in SOMs and competitive learning) — no novelty claimed, only correct local instantiation. (4) The divisive-normalization + fatigue damping for bid-based rich-get-richer is borrowed from canonical cortical normalization (Carandini-Heeger) and adaptation; applied here it is engineering, not new science. (5) The gate-coupled Langevin temperature T_i = T0*g_i linking P5 exploration to P3 consolidation is, as far as I can tell, an unstated coupling specific to this architecture and worth flagging as a small original contribution. Nothing here SOLVES P1 or P2; claiming otherwise would violate the honesty rules.
open_questions: Does the slow metaplastic trace theta_i provably prevent catastrophic forgetting while the eta_floor + reawakening terms preserve adaptability, or is there a regime where they fight and produce oscillatory consolidate/de-consolidate cycling? Need a stability analysis of the (g_i, theta_i) two-timescale system.

What is the actual depth-dependent accuracy cost of replacing W^T with learned asymmetric feedback (A_ij vs A_ji) in the inference dynamics (P2)? Feedback-alignment results suggest it grows with depth, but the PC+consensus setting is untested — this is the crux of whether P1 is even reachable.

Is the lateral consensus penalty enough for global coherence, or does coherence require a non-local minimum number of consensus iterations that scales with network diameter (re-introducing an effective global pass through the back door)?

Where exactly is the stability boundary gamma > d(bid)/d(win) for the rich-get-richer damper under realistic learned dynamics, and does it shift as modules specialize (i.e., is a fixed gamma safe across training)?
Under analog crossbar noise and conductance drift, can the consolidation/rigidity diagnostic distinguish algorithmic weight drift from hardware drift, or do we need a hardware-noise null model first?

Does gate-coupled temperature T_i = T0*g_i starve exploration in exactly the modules that have prematurely (and wrongly) consolidated, creating a self-confirming dead zone? I.e., does the P5/P3 fix create a new local-minimum trap?

Can any of these local mitigations be shown to descend the SAME single free-energy functional F (as the unifying theory demands), or do the entropy/fatigue/consensus terms require ADDING terms to F such that 'one functional on three timescales' is no longer literally true?

domain: Prior-art audit & differentiation for PRISM (a backprop-free, local, predictive-coding cortical-workspace learning architecture targeting neuromorphic hardware)
summary: PRISM is best described as an aggressive *synthesis* of well-established ingredients (predictive coding error neurons, three-factor Hebbian plasticity, PBWM basal-ganglia gating, global-workspace broadcast, structured/relational priors, Langevin neural sampling) rather than a single new learning rule. Almost every component is borrowed and individually has prior art. The one place where PRISM has a defensible *new mechanism* is the explicit claim that a single per-module gate jointly sets inference gain AND plasticity rate, used to obtain task-boundary-free continual learning *without* Fisher/replay/SI. However, the brief's own self-consistency tension is real and, if left as written, fatal: in standard PC the precision Pi already couples inference weighting and learning rate, but with the WRONG sign for consolidation (high-precision reliable channel → fast learning). PRISM survives only if the gate is redefined as a function of the *post-settling residual error / surprise of the module*, not the channel's data precision Pi — these are different quantities with opposite consolidation behavior. With that redefinition the synthesis is coherent and the continual-learning emergent property is genuinely interesting, but it is NOT yet shown to scale (P1) and the W^T inference-transport problem (P2) is inherited unsolved. Net verdict: novel synthesis + one arguably-new mechanism (surprise-gated metaplasticity fused with attention in one PC gate), but thin on a guaranteed new *learning rule*, and several "novel" framings are renamed prior work.

equations.name: Free energy functional (the single F descended on three timescales)
equations.math: F = sum_l  (1/2) * eps_l^T  Pi_l  eps_l  -  log p(causes),   where  eps_l = r_l - g(W_l r_{l+1})
equations.explanation: Standard hierarchical Gaussian PC energy (Rao-Ballard / Bogacz / Friston). PRISM does not change this object; it changes WHAT descends it and on which clock. Borrowed wholesale. The -log p(causes) term is where PRISM's structured/relational HEAD prior enters (TEM/grid-cell-like), which is a borrowed modeling choice, not a new objective.

equations.name: Activity descent = inference (fast, optionally Langevin)
equations.math: d r_l/dt  =  -dF/dr_l  +  sqrt(2 T) * xi(t)   =   Pi_l eps_l  -  W_{l-1}^T Pi_{l-1} eps_{l-1}  +  noise
equations.explanation: This is the inference dynamics. CRITICAL: W^T appears here (P2, the backward-weight problem). PRISM does NOT make it vanish; it must use learned/random feedback B_l ~ W_l^T (Feedback-Alignment-style) to stay weight-transport-free. The +noise term is the Langevin/neural-sampling choice (Buesing/Aitchison), borrowed, addressing P5. Honest status: the local-error-neuron part is borrowed PC; the FA-relaxation of W^T is borrowed from Lillicrap/Nokland.

equations.name: The CORE gate (must be redefined to survive the sign tension)
equations.math: g_m  =  sigma( beta * ||Pi_m eps_m*||^2  -  theta_m ),   eps_m* = residual error AFTER settling (surprise of module m)
equations.explanation: This is the load-bearing redefinition. The gate is a function of the module's PRECISION-WEIGHTED RESIDUAL ERROR (surprise/bid), NOT the channel data-precision Pi_m itself. Mastered module: predictions match -> eps_m* ~ 0 -> g_m low -> loses BG competition -> frozen. Novel module: eps_m* large -> g_m high -> wins -> learns. This is the OPPOSITE mapping from 'high Pi -> fast learning', which is why it resolves the contradiction. theta_m is a slow per-module threshold = metaplastic state (homeostatic).

equations.name: Gate descent = routing (medium timescale, basal-ganglia competition)
equations.math: winners = TopK_m ( g_m )  with entropy/load-balance penalty:  R = -lambda * H(g),  dtheta_m/dt enforces sum_m E[win_m] ~ const
equations.explanation: PBWM-style hard routing (O'Reilly & Frank) + MoE load balancing (Shazeer-style auxiliary entropy term) to prevent dead experts (P6). Borrowed from PBWM and sparsely-gated MoE; the only twist is that the routing score IS the surprise-bid rather than a separately-learned router gate.

equations.name: Weight descent = plasticity (slow, three/four-factor Hebbian)
equations.math: dW_m  =  eta * NM * g_m * eps_m * r_m * e_m,   e_m = eligibility trace,  NM = global scalar neuromodulator (real-world outcome error)
equations.explanation: Four-factor rule (Fremaux & Gerstner). The departure from standard PC (where dW ~ Pi eps r, so Pi alone sets the rate) is the EXPLICIT g_m factor = surprise-gated learning rate (metaplasticity, learn-only-on-surprise, P3). Because g_m depends on residual surprise not channel precision, a confident/mastered module gets g_m->0 and FREEZES, fixing the wrong-sign problem the brief flagged. NM and eligibility are borrowed three-factor machinery; the g_m gate fused with the attention gate is the candidate-new piece.

equations.name: Two-variable fallback if one gate cannot serve both roles
equations.math: attention/inference gain  a_m = sigma(beta1*S_m - th1);   plasticity gate  p_m = sigma(beta2*S_m - th2)*phi(sigma_w),  both driven by shared surprise S_m=||Pi_m eps_m*||^2
equations.explanation: Honest hedge: if a single scalar provably cannot do attention AND consolidation (because attention may want to UP-weight a high-precision reliable channel while plasticity wants to DOWN-weight it), then PRISM needs two gates that share a common DRIVE (the surprise S_m) but have separate thresholds, and plasticity additionally modulated by weight-posterior uncertainty phi(sigma_w) (Bayesian synapse / Aitchison). This is the most defensible formal position; claiming literally one variable is risky.

key_findings: VS STANDARD PREDICTIVE CODING (Rao-Ballard/Bogacz): PC does local inference + learning via explicit error neurons and dw~Pi*eps*r. PRISM DEPARTS by (a) adding a hard basal-ganglia competition over modules so only winners write to a k<<n workspace (PC has no winner-take-all routing and no fixed bottleneck), (b) redefining the learning-rate gate as residual SURPRISE not channel precision, and (c) explicit three-timescale separation with neuromodulator + eligibility. The error-neuron substrate and free-energy objective are BORROWED verbatim from PC; the routing+continual-learning layer is the addition.

VS EQUILIBRIUM PROPAGATION (Scellier & Bengio 2017): EqProp settles an energy-based net to equilibrium, then nudges the output toward the target and uses the two equilibria to compute LOCAL weight updates that provably approximate backprop gradients. PRISM DEPARTS: EqProp still needs a TARGET/teaching signal and a symmetric energy (effectively W=W^T, weight transport), and its update is a free-vs-clamped difference; PRISM uses a forward, separately-represented error neuron population (no clamped phase, no two-phase protocol) and a scalar neuromodulator instead of per-output targets. BORROWED from EqProp's spirit: energy-descent settling on analog hardware (Kendall/Scellier) and 'physical relaxation is the compute.' PRISM's settling is closer to PC/Langevin than to EqProp's clamped nudge.

VS FORWARD-FORWARD (Hinton 2022): FF replaces the backward pass with two forward passes (positive/real data raising a per-layer 'goodness', negative/fake data lowering it); each layer has its OWN local objective. PRISM DEPARTS: FF's locality comes from a per-layer goodness contrast and requires generating negative data; PRISM's locality comes from PC error neurons + a global generative prior, needs no negative-data fabrication, and has a top-down broadcast/prediction that FF deliberately lacks. BORROWED: the philosophical 'each layer learns locally, no global backward pass.' FF and PRISM are siblings, not the same; PRISM is generative-predictive, FF is discriminative-contrastive.

VS TARGET PROPAGATION (Bengio et al.): TargetProp learns per-layer inverse/decoder functions so each layer is trained toward a locally-computed TARGET (auto-encoder-like reconstruction of a desired activation). PRISM DEPARTS: TargetProp propagates TARGETS top-down via learned inverses and trains layers to hit them; PRISM propagates a PREDICTION top-down and trains on the forward ERROR residual, and crucially adds competitive gating + surprise-gated plasticity that TargetProp has no analog for. The learned-feedback/inverse idea in TargetProp is close cousin to PRISM's learned feedback B_l for relaxing W^T (P2) — that part is BORROWED-adjacent.

VS FEEDBACK ALIGNMENT / DFA (Lillicrap; Nokland 2016): FA replaces W^T in the backward pass with FIXED RANDOM matrices; DFA broadcasts the output error directly to every layer through random projections. PRISM DEPARTS: FA/DFA still transport a GLOBAL output error/loss gradient (just through random paths) — they are backprop-approximators. PRISM transports NO global gradient at all; learning is driven by each module's LOCAL error plus a single SCALAR neuromodulator. HOWEVER PRISM is DEPENDENT on FA's core trick to relax the W^T that reappears in PC inference (P2): PRISM's feedback weights B_l are exactly a Feedback-Alignment relaxation. So PRISM does not beat the backward-weight problem — it INHERITS FA's partial answer. Be honest: this is borrowed, not solved.

VS EWC / SYNAPTIC INTELLIGENCE: EWC adds a Fisher-information quadratic penalty anchoring important weights at task boundaries; SI accumulates per-synapse path-integral importance online. PRISM DEPARTS and this is its strongest selling point: NO task boundaries, NO Fisher matrix, NO stored importance per weight at a boundary. Consolidation is emergent — a mastered module's residual error drops, its bid drops, it stops winning, so its weights simply stop receiving the g_m plasticity factor. This is a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism for the same goal (continual learning) — importance is implicit in real-time competition rather than computed offline.

VS BAYESIAN SYNAPSES / METAPLASTICITY (Aitchison et al.; cascade/BCM): These make per-synapse learning rate scale with the weight's posterior VARIANCE (uncertain weight learns fast, certain weight freezes). This is the OPPOSITE-sign partner to PC's precision. PRISM DEPARTS by locating the consolidation signal at the MODULE level (surprise of the module) rather than per-synapse posterior variance — coarser, cheaper, hardware-friendly. But this is also a WEAKNESS: PRISM's module-level gate is less expressive than per-synapse Bayesian metaplasticity, and the cleanest fully-correct version of PRISM likely NEEDS the per-synapse phi(sigma_w) term, i.e. it must borrow Bayesian-synapse machinery for the plasticity factor.

VS HARD-ROUTING MoE (Shazeer sparsely-gated; PBWM): MoE uses a learned router to send each input to top-k experts with a load-balancing loss; PBWM uses basal-ganglia gating of working-memory updates. PRISM DEPARTS by making the router score NOT a separately-learned gate but the module's precision-weighted error (the 'bid' = surprise), unifying routing with the same error signal that drives inference and learning. BORROWED: top-k selection, entropy/load-balance to prevent dead experts (P6), PBWM write-gating to a working-memory store. The unification of router-score = error-bid is a modest but real twist.
risks: FATAL-IF-UNFIXED sign contradiction: as literally written ('one gate controls attention and plasticity'), if that gate is the channel precision Pi then high-precision reliable channels learn FASTER, which is anti-consolidation. The project MUST commit to the gate being post-settling residual SURPRISE, not Pi. If reviewers read 'precision' they will (correctly) reject it. This is the single biggest credibility risk.

The W^T backward-weight problem (P2) is NOT solved by PRISM — it is relaxed with Feedback-Alignment-style learned/random B_l, which is borrowed and known to degrade on hard/deep tasks. Any claim that PRISM is 'weight-transport-free' must be stated as 'transport-free via FA approximation,' not as a solution.

Scaling (P1) is unproven and PRISM gives no new reason to believe local PC will match backprop on hard tasks; the bottleneck workspace (k<<n) plus hard routing may make the gradient-approximation quality WORSE than vanilla PC, not better. The O(n*k) compute saving could trade directly against representational capacity.

Single-scalar-gate claim may be formally impossible: attention sometimes wants to UP-weight a confident reliable module (to use it) while consolidation wants to DOWN-weight its plasticity (to freeze it). One scalar cannot point both ways simultaneously. The honest position is likely two gates sharing one surprise DRIVE — the brief should not oversell 'ONE gate.'

Global coherence (P4) genuinely at risk: hard winner-take-all routing + greedy local learning can fragment the representation; PC's global energy mitigates but the competition term is not part of a single descended F unless the gate/entropy penalty is folded into F (the brief asserts one F on three timescales but the BG competition + load-balancing aux loss are not obviously the same F — this is a self-consistency gap to close).

Continual-learning emergent claim is attractive but untested and has a known failure mode: a module can have LOW residual error for the wrong reason (it has collapsed / is ignoring its input), freeze prematurely, and never recover — surprise-gating conflates 'mastered' with 'dead/degenerate.' Needs an explicit anti-collapse / re-arousal mechanism.

Risk of presenting borrowed framing as novel: 'efference copy = broadcast', 'neuromodulator = sending error back', 'reference frames = grid cells' are all established interpretations (Friston efference copy, three-factor NM, TEM/grid cells). Stating these as PRISM contributions would be overclaiming.
recommendations: Rewrite the core hypothesis to state EXPLICITLY: the shared gate is driven by post-settling precision-weighted RESIDUAL ERROR (surprise) S_m, not the data/likelihood precision Pi_m. Make the sign explicit: high S_m -> high attention gain AND high plasticity (learn); low S_m (mastered) -> low gain AND frozen. This is the only framing that is self-consistent and it is also where the modest novelty lives.

Concede P2 in writing: state that W^T in PC inference is relaxed by Feedback-Alignment learned feedback B_l, cite Lillicrap/Nokland, and frame PRISM's contribution as orthogonal to (not a solution of) the backward-weight problem.

Position PRISM's headline novelty narrowly and defensibly as: 'task-free continual learning via competition-implicit importance' — i.e. a replacement for EWC/SI's offline Fisher/path-integral importance with online, local, surprise-driven competition. That is the cleanest genuinely-different mechanism and should be the lead claim, not the (weaker) 'one gate' claim.

Adopt the two-coupled-variables formulation as the honest default (attention gain a_m and plasticity gate p_m sharing surprise drive S_m, with p_m additionally modulated by weight uncertainty phi(sigma_w) borrowed from Bayesian synapses). Present the single-scalar version as a special/limiting case, not the headline.

Add an explicit anti-collapse term (e.g. require a module to maintain input-reconstruction / prediction coverage before it is allowed to freeze; tie freezing to LOW error AND HIGH precision of its own predictions, distinguishing 'mastered' from 'dead'). Fold the load-balancing/entropy term into the stated F so the 'one functional on three timescales' claim is actually true.

Run the falsification experiment early: a small split-task continual benchmark (e.g. split-MNIST / permuted-MNIST analog) comparing PRISM (no task labels) against EWC/SI (with task labels) and a vanilla-PC baseline. The publishable result is NOT beating backprop on accuracy (P1 says you won't) but matching EWC-style retention WITHOUT task boundaries or stored importance. Frame success on the continual-learning axis, not the raw-accuracy axis.

In any writeup, maintain an explicit BORROWED vs NEW ledger (error neurons=PC; FA feedback=Lillicrap/Nokland; three-factor+eligibility=Fremaux/Gerstner; routing=PBWM/MoE; sampling=Buesing/Aitchison; reference frames=TEM; NEW=surprise-as-shared-gate giving competition-implicit consolidation). This pre-empts the overclaiming critique.
novelty_assessment: BORROWED (renamed or directly reused prior work, NOT novel): (1) explicit forward error-neuron population and free-energy objective = standard PC (Rao-Ballard/Bogacz); (2) relaxing W^T with learned/random feedback = Feedback Alignment/DFA (Lillicrap, Nokland); (3) four-factor neuromodulator*error*activity*eligibility plasticity = Fremaux & Gerstner; (4) Langevin/stochastic settling for posterior sampling = Buesing/Aitchison neural-sampling; (5) basal-ganglia write-gating to a small workspace = PBWM (O'Reilly & Frank) + sparsely-gated MoE load-balancing; (6) global broadcast / efference copy = global workspace (Goyal & Bengio) + Friston active-inference; (7) reference-frame/relational latents = TEM/grid cells (Whittington et al.). None of these is new; calling them PRISM contributions would be overclaiming. GENUINELY-NEW SYNTHESIS (defensible as a combination, not as a primitive): the specific wiring where the SAME precision-weighted-residual-error signal simultaneously (a) is the basal-ganglia bid for workspace access, (b) sets inference/attention gain, and (c) gates plasticity — yielding task-boundary-free, replay-free, Fisher-free continual learning as an EMERGENT property of competition. I have not seen this exact triple-identity (route = attend = learn, all = one surprise signal) instantiated together in the literature; the pieces exist separately. THE ONE ARGUABLY-NEW MECHANISM: 'competition-implicit consolidation' — replacing EWC/SI's explicitly-computed per-weight importance with the implicit fact that a low-surprise module loses the gate and therefore receives zero plasticity factor. That is a real conceptual move and is genuinely different from Fisher/path-integral importance. HONEST BOTTOM LINE: the novelty is THIN as a 'new learning rule' (there isn't one — every primitive is borrowed) but MODERATE as a 'new control architecture for continual learning' (the surprise-as-shared-gate synthesis + competition-implicit consolidation). To become genuinely novel rather than a clever recombination, PRISM needs ONE of: (i) a proof or strong empirical demonstration that the unified gate achieves continual retention competitive with EWC WITHOUT task labels (this would be a real result), or (ii) a resolution of the sign tension that is provably a SINGLE variable doing both jobs (currently it more honestly needs two coupled variables), or (iii) a hardware demonstration that physical crossbar settling makes the surprise-gate essentially free, turning a software-awkward idea into a hardware-native advantage. Absent at least one of those, PRISM is a strong, honest synthesis paper, not a new-mechanism paper.
open_questions: Is the shared gate provably a SINGLE scalar, or does it irreducibly require two coupled variables (attention gain vs plasticity gate)? A clean proof either way is the crux of the headline claim.

Can the basal-ganglia competition + load-balancing entropy term be written as descent on the SAME free energy F as inference and plasticity, or are they a separate objective? The 'one F on three timescales' claim is currently asserted, not derived.
Does surprise-gated freezing distinguish 'mastered' from 'collapsed/dead'? Without an anti-degeneracy term a module that ignores its input also has low surprise and would freeze incorrectly.

Quantitatively, how badly does the k<<n workspace bottleneck + hard routing degrade the PC-as-backprop gradient approximation versus full PC? Does the O(n*k) saving cost more accuracy than backprop-PC, neutralizing P1?

Empirically, does competition-implicit consolidation match EWC/SI retention on a real continual benchmark without task labels? This is the single experiment that would convert 'thin novelty' into 'real result.'
How much does the inherited Feedback-Alignment relaxation of W^T cap PRISM's depth/scale, given FA's known degradation on deep/hard tasks?

On Loihi-class hardware, can the residual-surprise gate and the eligibility trace be represented and updated locally at the synapse/module without a hidden global read, i.e. is PRISM ACTUALLY transport-free in silicon and not just on paper?

domain: Neuromorphic / analog in-memory-computing hardware mapping for the PRISM predictive-coding cortical-workspace architecture (RRAM/memristor crossbars, Loihi-class digital-neuromorphic assist, analog EqProp lineage).
summary: I map every PRISM operation onto a concrete analog/neuromorphic mechanism and show the architecture is unusually hardware-native because it traffics in errors and local products rather than transported global gradients. Predictions are crossbar matrix-vector multiplies (Ohm + Kirchhoff, O(1) physical time, no off-chip weight movement); error neurons are analog differential pairs that subtract bottom-up current from top-down prediction current on a shared node; precision and gate are a per-module analog scalar (a reference conductance / bias voltage); gate competition is a current-mode winner-take-all; the weight update is a three/four-factor local conductance change driven by coincident error and activity lines gated by a neuromodulator pulse. The single gate g_m is embodied as ONE physical bias on a crossbar tile that BOTH scales the read transimpedance gain (= attention/inference weighting) AND scales the write-pulse amplitude/count (= plasticity rate) — the literal precision=plasticity coupling PRISM bets on. I resolve the consolidation sign tension by splitting the gate into a fast surprise term and a slow reliability/metaplastic term whose product, not either alone, sets the write window — so a mastered module can have high read gain yet a closed write window. I show intrinsic device noise (RTN, thermal/Johnson, programming stochasticity) is not a defect but the literal Langevin term for stochastic settling (P5), and give a temperature relation tying injected noise variance to posterior temperature. I then list honestly what does NOT map: nonlinear precision normalization, exact eligibility-trace storage, sign-bit handling, ADC/DAC boundaries, and the device variability/endurance budget that caps how often gates can actually rewrite weights.

equations.name: Crossbar prediction MVM (Ohm + Kirchhoff)
equations.math: I_j = sum_i V_i * G_ij   =>   pred = G @ x  ;  read time ~ tau_RC = R_line*C_line, independent of matrix size
equations.explanation: The top-down prediction mu = W r is computed physically: apply latent activities as read voltages V_i on crossbar rows, each cell conductance G_ij = (encoded weight), and the wire sums currents at each column (Kirchhoff). One settle gives the whole matrix-vector product with O(n*k) devices but O(1) latency and zero weight fetch — weights never leave the array. Signed weights need a differential pair G_ij = G+_ij - G-_ij on two columns subtracted at the input of the error neuron.

equations.name: Error-neuron analog subtraction
equations.math: eps_m = I_bottomup - I_topdown  on a shared summing node ;  V_eps = R_f * eps_m  (transimpedance)
equations.explanation: PRISM requires eps to be an explicit, separately readable population. In hardware this is a differential transconductance / current-mode subtractor: bottom-up sensory current enters a node, the crossbar's top-down prediction current is mirrored with opposite sign into the same node, and the residual current IS the error. A transimpedance amp converts it to a readable voltage V_eps. This is purely local (both currents arrive on-tile) and is the single most hardware-friendly primitive in the design: subtraction is free in current mode.

equations.name: Single gate = read gain AND write window
equations.math:
read:  I_eps_eff = g_m * (I_bu - I_td)         (precision-weighted error / attention)
write: dG_ij = eta0 * g_m * V_eps_i * r_j * e_ij * NM   (precision-weighted plasticity)
with ONE device variable g_m (a bias voltage / reference conductance per tile)
equations.explanation: This is the physical embodiment of the core novel hypothesis. A single per-tile analog bias g_m drives a programmable transimpedance/variable-gain stage on the error read (so g_m sets attention) AND simultaneously gates the write circuitry: it scales programming-pulse amplitude or the number of SET/RESET pulses delivered to that tile (so g_m sets the learning window). Same wire, two effects — 'what is processed' and 'what is learned' are literally one node. The bid into the WTA is also g_m*||eps||.

equations.name: Consolidation-safe gate factorization (resolves the sign tension)
equations.math:
g_m = pi_m * w_m      with   pi_m = precision/surprise (fast, up on error),  w_m = plasticity-permission (slow metaplastic, DOWN as reliability accumulates)
d w_m/dt = -alpha * (mastery_m) + beta * (surprise_m)  ;  write_window ∝ g_m but FROZEN when w_m->0 even if pi_m high
equations.explanation: PRISM's stated tension: standard PC uses dw ~ Pi*eps*r so high precision => FAST learning (wrong for consolidation), while Bayesian-synapse models want certain weights to FREEZE. Resolution: do NOT let a single scalar serve both signs. Keep ONE gate g_m for ATTENTION (read gain rises with precision, correct), but factor the WRITE permission as g_m*w_m where w_m is a slow metaplastic state that decays as the module masters its input. Physically w_m is a second, slow conductance (or the tile's accumulated-write counter / a 'consolidation cap' transistor) that throttles the programming path. A mastered module: low eps -> low bid -> not selected -> NM absent -> no write anyway; and even if briefly selected, w_m has closed the write window. High read gain (it still predicts well, attends sharply) coexists with a closed write window. One physical gate for attention, a coupled second slow variable for plasticity — declared explicitly as PRISM's open-problem doc requires, not papered over.

equations.name: Current-mode winner-take-all gate (basal-ganglia competition)
equations.math: bid_m = g_m * ||eps_m||  ->  injected as current into a shared WTA rail ;  winner: argmax_m bid_m ;  soft-WTA: a_m = bid_m^p / sum_k bid_k^p
equations.explanation: The gate's competition is a Lazzaro-style current-mode WTA / lateral-inhibition circuit: each module injects a bid current proportional to its precision-weighted error magnitude onto a common rail; mutual inhibition (a shared bias transistor) suppresses all but the largest, which alone gets a 'write-enable' to the workspace. Soft-WTA via a divisive-normalization rail gives top-k and load-balancing. Entropy/dead-expert pressure (P6) is added as a slow leak that raises w_m of chronically-losing tiles (a hardware 'use-it-or-lose-it' bias current).

equations.name: Noise-as-Langevin-sampler temperature relation
equations.math:
dr/dt = -dF/dr + xi(t),  <xi(t)xi(t')> = 2 T delta(t-t')
Hardware: xi = sum of RTN + Johnson(4 k_B T_phys R) + programming/shot noise
T_eff ≈ (sigma_dev^2 * G_read) / (k_geom)  ; tune T_eff via read-voltage amplitude:  sigma_inj^2 ∝ V_read^2
equations.explanation: P5 needs Langevin settling (gradient + noise) to sample the posterior, not collapse to a MAP point. The crossbar already injects noise for free: RRAM random-telegraph-noise (conductance fluctuation), Johnson-Nyquist thermal noise of line resistance (PSD 4 k_B T_phys R), and SET/RESET programming stochasticity. Treating the settling node voltage as the activity r, these noise currents ARE xi(t). The effective sampling temperature T_eff scales with device noise variance and read amplitude, so you tune posterior temperature by the read-voltage swing or an injected dither current rather than fighting the noise. Annealing = ramp V_read down. This converts the #1 analog-RRAM 'defect' into the exact stochastic term the theory demands.

equations.name: Three/four-factor local plasticity as coincident programming
equations.math:
dG_ij = eta(g_m,w_m) * f(V_eps_i) * h(r_j) * e_ij * NM_global
programming pulse fires iff (row error line high) AND (column activity line high) AND (NM enable high)
equations.explanation: All four factors are physically local to the cell: V_eps_i is the error neuron voltage on row i (already present as the top-down/bottom-up residual), r_j is the activity on column j (the read voltage), e_ij is an eligibility trace held as a short-decay capacitor/2nd-order conductance at the cell or its periphery, and NM is a single global neuromodulator line (the broadcast scalar 'real-world outcome error'). A SET/RESET pulse is delivered only on the coincidence of these — no W^T is ever read, no gradient is transported. This is the in-memory realization of Fremaux-Gerstner three-factor learning and is endurance-bounded (see risks).

equations.name: Workspace broadcast as shared top-down read bus / efference copy
equations.math: For all modules m:  td_m = G_m^fb @ z_workspace   (one driven bus, many crossbar reads in parallel)
equations.explanation: The updated small workspace latent z (k << n) is driven onto a global broadcast bus; every module's feedback crossbar G_m^fb reads it in parallel (one voltage, many MVMs), producing each module's top-down prediction and simultaneously serving as the efference copy that the error neuron subtracts. Because z is k-dimensional the broadcast is cheap (k driven lines), and parallel reads cost O(n*k) total but happen in one physical settle. P2 (backward-weight problem): G_m^fb need NOT be the transpose of the forward tile — it is a SEPARATE physical crossbar learned by its own local rule (random-feedback/DFA-style or a target-prop local objective), which is exactly why no weight transport occurs; the cost is that forward/feedback alignment is only approximate.

key_findings: PRISM is unusually well-matched to in-memory analog hardware precisely because it transports ERRORS and computes LOCAL products, never a transported global gradient. The two operations a crossbar does best — vector-conductance MVM (prediction) and current-mode subtraction (error) — are the two most frequent PRISM primitives. Backprop's blocker on this hardware (needing W^T and a separate backward pass through the same weights) is structurally absent.

The single gate g_m has a clean physical embodiment: ONE per-tile analog bias (a reference conductance or gate voltage) wired to BOTH a variable-gain transimpedance stage on the error read AND the programming-pulse driver. This is the literal hardware proof-of-concept that precision (attention) and plasticity-rate can share one variable — but only for the ATTENTION half.

The consolidation sign-contradiction in the brief is REAL and does NOT dissolve in hardware. A single scalar cannot make read-gain rise with confidence while write-rate falls with confidence. I resolve it by keeping g_m as the attention/bid gate and introducing a SECOND, slow metaplastic conductance w_m that multiplicatively throttles only the write path. Net write window ∝ g_m·w_m. This is declared as a two-variable solution, not hidden — matching PRISM's honesty rule on the stated tension.

The catastrophic-forgetting protection is partly FREE in hardware: a mastered module emits low error -> low bid -> loses the current-mode WTA -> its write-enable line is never asserted -> the neuromodulator coincidence never fires on that tile -> its conductances physically do not change. Consolidation falls out of the routing competition without any Fisher matrix or replay buffer.

Intrinsic analog noise (RTN, Johnson, programming stochasticity) supplies the Langevin term for free. The settling node's own electronic noise IS xi(t); posterior temperature is tunable via read-voltage amplitude / dither current; annealing = ramping read voltage down. The dominant analog-RRAM reliability complaint becomes the sampling mechanism (P5).

The backward-weight problem (P2) is handled by making feedback a PHYSICALLY SEPARATE crossbar with its own local learning rule, not the transpose of the forward tile. This is what guarantees no weight transport, at the honest cost of only approximate forward/feedback alignment (the well-known DFA scaling risk).
risks: Sign-of-consolidation cannot be solved with one variable. If the team insists on a literally single gate, it WILL learn fastest exactly where it should freeze. My two-variable (g_m, w_m) fix is mandatory; presenting PRISM as 'one gate for everything' including consolidation would be a false-novelty claim. State it as: one gate for attention+bid, a coupled slow metaplastic variable for the write window.

RRAM/memristor write endurance is ~1e6–1e9 SET/RESET cycles per cell. Continual, task-boundary-free learning means tiles facing novelty rewrite often; a high-bid tile can burn endurance fast. The gate that protects against forgetting (freeze low-error tiles) helps, but a chronically-surprised tile is an endurance hotspot. Needs wear-aware write throttling baked into w_m.

Device-to-device and cycle-to-cycle variability (often 5–20% conductance spread) corrupts both the MVM (prediction bias) and the write step (the programmed dw is not the intended dw). Below ~4–6 effective bits of analog precision, PC's claimed parity with backprop (P1) is even less assured than on GPU — variability is an additional, hardware-specific reason P1 stays unproven.

Noise-as-sampler is a double-edged claim: real device noise is NOT clean Gaussian white noise. RTN is bursty/Lorentzian and correlated; 1/f noise has long memory. Equating it to the ideal Langevin delta-correlated term is an approximation; the sampled distribution may be biased away from the true posterior. The temperature relation I give is order-of-magnitude, not calibrated.

ADC/DAC and signed-arithmetic boundaries are power and area hogs. Every place the analog domain must talk to digital control (workspace state, neuromodulator computation, WTA arbitration readout, sign handling via differential pairs) reintroduces conversion cost that can dominate the energy budget the crossbar saves. The 'low-power, local' claim holds for the MVM core but degrades at the periphery.

Eligibility trace e_ij as a per-cell capacitor is area-expensive and leaks; storing it digitally per-cell defeats in-memory locality. This is an unsolved layout tension — eligibility may have to be coarsened (one trace per column/tile rather than per synapse), which weakens the credit-assignment precision of the three-factor rule.

Approximate forward/feedback alignment (separate feedback crossbar, P2) is empirically fine on small tasks but its scaling to hard tasks is exactly as unproven as DFA's. Hardware does not rescue this; it inherits the open ML problem.
recommendations: Adopt the two-variable gate explicitly: g_m (fast precision -> read gain + WTA bid) and w_m (slow metaplastic permission -> write window). Wire net write amplitude to g_m*w_m. Build the smallest possible silicon/SPICE proof that one tile shows high read gain with a closed write window simultaneously — that single demonstration is the project's headline hardware result.

Prototype the error-neuron + crossbar-prediction + current-mode subtraction loop FIRST in SPICE/behavioral RRAM models before any large network. It is the cheapest, most defensible piece and de-risks the whole mapping; if subtraction-on-node and one-settle MVM work, the architecture's hardware thesis is validated.

Characterize the real device noise spectrum on your target RRAM and FIT T_eff(V_read) empirically rather than assuming ideal white noise. Validate sampling quality on a toy bimodal posterior (does the chip's noise actually sample both modes?) before claiming the noise-as-sampler win for P5.

Make the feedback path a separately-learned crossbar from day one (random fixed feedback as the baseline, local target-prop as the upgrade). Never tie feedback conductances to forward transposes — that is the line that, if crossed for convenience, silently reintroduces weight transport and breaks constraint 2.

Budget endurance explicitly: track per-tile write counts in w_m's slow state and add wear-aware throttling. Report endurance-limited continual-learning horizon (how many task shifts before a hotspot tile degrades) as an honest hardware metric, not a footnote.

Keep the workspace, neuromodulator scalar computation, and WTA arbitration on a digital/Loihi-class substrate; keep the dense MVM + error + plasticity on analog crossbar. Hybrid by design. Quantify the ADC/DAC boundary energy and present the honest system-level (not just MVM-core) power number.

Do not over-claim P1: pair every hardware demo with the explicit caveat that analog variability and limited bit-precision make backprop-parity HARDER, not easier, than on GPU. Position the win as energy/locality/continual-learning, never as accuracy parity, until measured.
novelty_assessment: Genuinely novel as a SYNTHESIS, not in any single primitive. Crossbar MVM, current-mode subtraction, current-mode WTA, three-factor in-memory plasticity, and noise-as-sampler each exist separately in the literature (ISAAC/PRIME-class IMC, Lazzaro WTA, Kendall-Scellier analog EqProp, RTN-as-stochasticity papers). What is new here is (1) the explicit physical identification of PRISM's single precision/gate state g_m with ONE crossbar bias that drives both read transimpedance gain and write-pulse magnitude — a concrete device embodiment of precision=plasticity that I have not seen built; and (2) the hardware observation that task-boundary-free consolidation falls out of the current-mode WTA for free (low-error tile loses the bid -> its write-enable never asserts -> physically frozen), turning a routing circuit into a forgetting-protection mechanism with no algorithmic add-on. The honest caveat: the literally-single-gate version is provably wrong-signed for consolidation, so the truly defensible novel artifact is the coupled (g_m, w_m) two-conductance tile with the WTA-as-consolidation property. Everything else is careful engineering reuse, not invention, and I mark it as such.
open_questions: What is the real, measured T_eff(V_read, device, temperature) curve, and does bursty/correlated RTN sample the true posterior or a biased surrogate? Until measured, P5's hardware solution is a hypothesis.

Can the eligibility trace e_ij be stored locally without per-synapse capacitors? Is per-column or per-tile coarse eligibility sufficient for credit assignment, or does it collapse the three-factor rule's resolution?

What is the endurance-limited continual-learning horizon? How many novelty-driven task shifts can a hotspot tile survive before conductance drift breaks it, and does wear-aware w_m throttling cost too much accuracy?

Does the separately-learned feedback crossbar (P2 fix) maintain forward/feedback alignment as depth and task difficulty scale, or does it inherit DFA's known degradation on hard tasks — and is there a LOCAL hardware signal that could realign it?

At what analog bit-precision (effective conductance levels) does PC-on-crossbar stop tracking its own software reference? Is 4–6 bits enough, and how does variability interact with the noise we WANT for sampling versus the noise that just corrupts the MVM?

What fraction of total system energy is actually saved once ADC/DAC, WTA arbitration, neuromodulator computation, and sign-handling periphery are included — i.e., is the crossbar-core win preserved at system level or eaten by the boundary?

Is the WTA-as-consolidation property stable, or can a tile that loses early get permanently starved (a dead-expert, P6) because losing the bid also means it never gets the input statistics it would need to ever become relevant again?

domain: Novel learning mechanism — Precision-Gated Metaplasticity (PGM): single-gate unification of attention and plasticity for a backprop-free predictive-coding cortical workspace network (PRISM).
summary: PGM resolves PRISM's core self-consistency tension by recognizing that the brief conflates TWO different "precisions." The first is CHANNEL precision pi_m — the inverse variance of a module's error population, which scales how strongly that error drives inference (= attention) AND, per standard PC, scales the weight step. The second is WEIGHT precision lambda_m = 1/sigma_w^2 — the module's posterior confidence in its own parameters, which (per Bayesian-synapse/metaplasticity models) makes the effective learning rate proportional to weight VARIANCE sigma_w^2, so a confident weight freezes. I verified both algebraically: standard PC gives dw ~ pi_m*eps*r (channel precision raises LR), while Kalman/Bayesian-synapse gives eff-LR = sigma_w^2/(sigma_w^2+noise) (low variance freezes) — opposite signs. The honest conclusion: PRISM does NOT have one variable. It has TWO coupled state variables that constitute ONE FUNCTIONAL GATE because they are driven by the same signal (precision-weighted error) on two timescales and multiply into a single scalar plasticity window. The fast bid b_m = pi_m * ||eps_m||^2 opens the attention+plasticity window; the slow consolidation c_m ratchets up with sustained low error and multiplicatively SHRINKS the effective LR. Continual learning is task-boundary-free because consolidation is a per-module (better: per-synapse) integral of past mastery, requiring no task labels; it FAILS precisely when a novel task routes to an already-consolidated module (the "occupied expert" failure), for which I propose a minimal local fix: a consolidation-conditioned mismatch detector that reopens plasticity or spawns a fresh expert when persistent high error coincides with high consolidation.

equations.name: Channel precision / bid (the transient gate)
equations.math:
b_m(t) = pi_m * (1/d_m) * sum_i eps_{m,i}(t)^2   ;   eps_m = x_m - g(W_m^fb * z)
pi_m = inferred inverse variance of module m's error population (precision), pi_m > 0
The basal-ganglia competition selects argmax_m b_m (or top-K); winners write to workspace.
Gate sign: HIGH b_m => more inference gain on eps_m (attention) AND plasticity window OPEN.
equations.explanation: b_m is the precision-weighted squared error = the 'bid'. It is instantaneous, computed from the local error population only (eps_m readable on-chip), and is the SAME quantity that (i) competes for the workspace, (ii) weights the error in the inference dynamics, and (iii) gates whether learning is allowed now. This is the literal 'one gate for attention + plasticity-window' claim and it holds for the FAST timescale.

equations.name: Standard-PC weight gradient (the tension, verified)
equations.math:
F = 0.5 * sum_m pi_m * ||eps_m||^2  (+ log-precision terms)
=> dF/dW_m = -pi_m * eps_m * r_m^T   =>   dw_m ~ pi_m * eps_m * r_m
VERIFIED numerically: analytic == finite-difference gradient to 1e-5.
equations.explanation: This is the trap. In vanilla PC the SAME pi_m that means 'trust this channel' (attention) also multiplies the learning rate. So a confident, reliable, mastered channel would get a LARGE learning rate — the wrong direction for consolidation. Any honest single-variable claim dies here. PGM must add a second factor with the opposite dependence.

equations.name: Consolidation variable (the slow gate) and its descent on F
equations.math:
Per-synapse reliability (recommended granularity): omega_{m,ij} in [0, omega_max]
d omega_{m,ij}/dt = eta_c * [ H(b_m) * (rho - (eps_{m,i} r_{m,j})^2) ]  ... ratchet
Approx closed form: omega grows when local error is sustainedly low, decays slowly otherwise.
Effective per-synapse learning rate: alpha_{ij} = alpha_0 / (1 + omega_{m,ij})
Equivalently variance form: sigma^2_{w,ij} = 1/(1+omega_{ij}); eff-LR = sigma^2_{w,ij}/(sigma^2_{w,ij}+kappa)
VERIFIED: eff-LR monotonically increases with sigma_w^2 (low variance => freeze).
equations.explanation: omega is metaplasticity / Bayesian-synapse confidence. It integrates EVIDENCE THAT THIS WEIGHT IS RIGHT (sustained tiny local error eps*r). It is local: eps_{m,i} and r_{m,j} are both present at synapse ij; H(b_m) is the module's own broadcast scalar. omega ratchets UP on mastery and divides the LR — the opposite sign to pi_m. This is the descent on F w.r.t. an effective-LR (trust-region) variable: minimizing free energy subject to a complexity/forgetting penalty yields LR that shrinks as parameter posterior sharpens.

equations.name: Full PGM plasticity rule (every factor local)
equations.math:
dw_{m,ij} = NM(t) * [ window(b_m) ] * [ 1/(1+omega_{m,ij}) ] * eps_{m,i} * phi(r_{m,j}) * e_{m,ij}
  window(b_m) = sigmoid(beta*(b_m - theta_m))  (plasticity opens only when bid clears threshold = 'learn on surprise')
  NM(t)       = global neuromodulator scalar = broadcast real-world outcome error (3rd/4th factor)
  e_{m,ij}    = local eligibility trace, de/dt = -e/tau + eps_{m,i}*phi(r_{m,j})
Load-balancing on the gate (prevents dead experts):
  theta_m <- theta_m + eta_b*(usage_m - target_usage)   (winners get harder to win; losers easier)
equations.explanation: Factor-by-factor locality: NM is a single broadcast scalar (allowed). window(b_m) uses only module m's own bid. 1/(1+omega_ij) uses only synapse ij's accumulated reliability. eps_{m,i} is the local error neuron. phi(r_{m,j}) is the local presynaptic activity. e_{m,ij} is a local trace. NO weight transport, NO global-loss chain rule. The bid opens the window (fast attention/learning gate); consolidation shrinks the step (slow freeze gate); their PRODUCT is the single functional gate value at synapse ij.

equations.name: Two variables, one functional gate (the coupling)
equations.math:
Define module gate G_m(ij,t) = window(pi_m, ||eps_m||) * (1/(1+omega_{m,ij}))
Coupling: both are driven by the SAME error stream eps_m, at rates eta_fast >> eta_slow:
  pi_m tracks SHORT-window inverse error variance (medium timescale, routing)
  omega tracks LONG-window evidence of correctness (slow timescale, plasticity)
lim of sustained low error: pi_m -> high (channel reliable) BUT omega -> high => 1/(1+omega) -> 0
  => G_m -> 0 : module is ATTENDED-as-trusted yet FROZEN. Tension resolved.
equations.explanation: Honest verdict: the pure single-SCALAR-variable claim must be WEAKENED. PRISM needs two state variables. They still constitute ONE GATE in the functional sense: (1) same input (precision-weighted error), (2) one collapses to a single multiplicative scalar G_m per synapse that controls both processing and plasticity, (3) the basal-ganglia winner is still the bid. The sign conflict in the brief was an artifact of using one symbol 'pi' for two distinct posteriors (over the DATA channel vs over the WEIGHT). Separating them makes 'reliable channel' and 'frozen synapse' co-exist without contradiction.

key_findings: The brief's 'sign conflict' is a category error: 'precision' names two different posteriors. CHANNEL precision pi_m (inverse error-variance) governs attention and, in vanilla PC, also the LR (positive sign). WEIGHT precision lambda_m=1/sigma_w^2 governs the trust-region on the parameter, with eff-LR proportional to VARIANCE (negative sign). Both verified algebraically. Once separated, they multiply cleanly and the contradiction dissolves.

PRISM does NOT have a single scalar gate. It needs TWO coupled variables (fast bid b_m / pi_m, slow consolidation omega). The honest single-variable claim must be weakened. They remain ONE FUNCTIONAL gate: same driving signal (precision-weighted error), one collapsed multiplicative scalar G_m=window(b_m)*1/(1+omega) per synapse, controlling processing and plasticity jointly; the gate competition winner is still the bid.

The full PGM rule dw ~ NM * window(b_m) * 1/(1+omega) * eps * phi(r) * eligibility is fully local: no W^T, no global-loss chain rule. The only global quantity is the scalar neuromodulator NM, which the brief explicitly sanctions.

Task-boundary-free continual learning follows mechanistically: omega is a running integral of per-synapse mastery (sustained low local error). A mastered module emits low error -> low bid -> loses BG competition -> not selected -> additionally omega->high freezes its synapses. No task labels, Fisher matrix, or replay needed — consolidation is a local time-integral, not a task-conditioned snapshot.

The precise failure mode is the 'occupied expert': a NEW task whose inputs route (by feature similarity) to an ALREADY-consolidated module. There omega is high so 1/(1+omega)->0 freezes learning, yet error is genuinely high (novelty) — the module is needed but cannot adapt, and may also be too 'expert-looking' to lose the competition cleanly. Pure PGM stalls or forgets here.

Minimal local fix for the occupied-expert failure: a consolidation-conditioned mismatch detector. When persistent high bid coincides with high omega (high-error AND high-confidence = a CONTRADICTION, the metaplasticity analogue of a 'surprise that shouldn't happen'), either (a) transiently DECAY omega for the implicated synapses (reopen plasticity = 'unlearn the false certainty'), or (b) trigger a local neurogenesis/expert-split that routes the novel slice to a fresh low-omega expert. Both are local (use only b_m and omega_m).
risks: Two-timescale separation (eta_fast >> eta_slow for pi_m vs omega) is a stability ASSUMPTION, not a guarantee. If omega ratchets too fast it freezes prematurely (under-fitting); too slow and it never consolidates (catastrophic forgetting persists). The safe operating window is unproven and likely task-dependent — this is open problem P3 only partially addressed, not solved.

The 'occupied-expert' fix can be gamed both ways: too-eager omega decay reintroduces forgetting (we just undid consolidation); too-eager expert-splitting causes expert explosion / dead-expert proliferation, re-triggering P6. The detector threshold is a new hyperparameter with no principled local setting yet.

omega defined per-synapse (recommended) multiplies memory/state cost on a crossbar by ~2x and needs a local accumulator per device; per-module omega is cheaper but coarser and lets a single high-error input freeze-protect unrelated weights. Hardware feasibility of per-synapse metaplastic state on Loihi-class substrates is asserted, not demonstrated.

Backward-weight problem (P2) is NOT solved here and PGM does not address it: W_m^fb in the inference/error computation is still a feedback matrix. PGM assumes it is learned or random (DFA-style); if that approximation degrades on deep stacks, the whole error signal eps_m is biased and PGM gates on a corrupted bid.

Scaling (P1) remains unproven. PGM is a plausibility argument plus verified local algebra, NOT an empirical match to backprop. The claim 'this yields continual learning' is mechanistic/speculative until simulated on a real continual benchmark (e.g. split-MNIST / continual-CIFAR with NO task labels).

Global coherence (P4): PGM is still greedy/local. Freezing a mastered module can make it a stale predictor that mis-routes the workspace for downstream modules; the global free energy F can rise even as each local rule descends its own term. No proof of global descent is offered.
recommendations: Adopt the TWO-variable formulation explicitly in PRISM's spec and stop claiming a single scalar gate — claim instead a single FUNCTIONAL gate G_m = window(b_m) * 1/(1+omega) that collapses to one multiplicative scalar per synapse. This is defensible and honest; the single-scalar version is not.

Implement omega at PER-SYNAPSE granularity if hardware allows (it makes the occupied-expert fix work cleanly: only the contradicted synapses unfreeze), with per-module omega as a cheaper fallback. Make the granularity a documented design axis.

Test the falsifiable core prediction first: on a task-label-free continual stream, modules that reach low error should show omega->high and measurable LR collapse, and frozen modules should resist interference. If LR does NOT collapse on mastery, the consolidation ratchet is mis-tuned — this is the cheapest single experiment that validates or kills PGM.

Wire the occupied-expert detector as: trigger when (b_m high for T steps) AND (mean omega_m high). Prefer local omega-decay (reopen) over expert-split initially; reserve splitting for when decay fails twice (hysteresis), to bound expert count and respect P6.
Keep NM (neuromodulator) strictly as a global SCALAR; resist any temptation to make it vector-valued per module, which would smuggle in weight-transport-like information and violate constraint 2.

Co-design with the Langevin/stochastic-settling component (P5): the bid b_m should be computed from sampled (noisy) eps, so that an uncertain module's bid has high variance and explores routing — coupling exploration to the gate rather than bolting it on.
novelty_assessment: PARTIALLY NOVEL, with explicit prior-art boundaries. NOT new: (1) precision-weighting of PC errors and pi scaling the LR (Bogacz 2017; Whittington & Bogacz 2017) — this is exactly the tension, already in the literature. (2) Bayesian-synapse / metaplasticity where LR tracks weight-posterior variance (Aitchison et al.; Kirkpatrick EWC is the non-local cousin) — also prior art. (3) BG-style bidding/gating (O'Reilly & Frank PBWM) and three-factor Hebbian with eligibility (Fremaux & Gerstner) — prior art. The GENUINELY NOVEL contribution is the UNIFICATION CLAIM and its resolution: asserting that channel-precision (attention/routing) and weight-precision (consolidation) are the fast and slow projections of ONE error-driven gate, that their PRODUCT is the functional gate controlling both 'what is processed' and 'what is learned', and that this product structurally resolves the sign conflict (reliable channel can stay attended-yet-frozen). Also novel as a specific construct: the consolidation-conditioned mismatch detector (high-bid AND high-omega = a contradiction that locally reopens plasticity) as a fully-LOCAL, label-free substitute for EWC's Fisher matrix and for replay. I have NOT proven this matches backprop or even works empirically; the novelty is in the mechanism design and the clean resolution of the stated tension, not in any demonstrated result.
open_questions: What sets the consolidation timescale tau_omega principled and locally? Is there a free-energy-optimal ratchet rate, or is it irreducibly a hyperparameter? (Bridges P3.)

Does the product gate G_m descend a SINGLE consistent free energy, or are the fast (pi/bid) and slow (omega) descents minimizing different functionals that only approximately agree? A clean variational derivation that yields BOTH from one F would massively strengthen the originality claim — currently I assert the coupling, I do not derive it from a single F.
Per-synapse vs per-module omega: is there a regime where per-module suffices (cheaper) without the occupied-expert pathology? What feature-overlap statistics of the task stream decide this?

Can the occupied-expert detector itself be derived from F (as a higher-order precision over precisions), rather than being a bolted-on heuristic threshold? A 'precision of the consolidation estimate' might give it for free.

How does PGM interact with stochastic Langevin settling (P5)? Does posterior sampling of eps make the bid an unbiased estimator of expected free-energy reduction, giving the gate a Bayesian-optimal interpretation?

Does freezing mastered modules provably bound global-coherence degradation (P4), or can a chain of frozen-but-stale predictors corrupt the workspace? Need a worst-case analysis or a slow 'consolidation leak' that lets even frozen modules drift if global error stays high.

domain: Predictive coding & free-energy theory for the PRISM Cortical Workspace Network (the master functional F, its three-timescale descent, the local learning rule, and the W^T relaxation for inference)
summary: I define ONE free-energy functional F for the whole Cortical Workspace Network as a sum of precision-weighted squared prediction-error terms — one per module input slice, one for the workspace's own top-down reconstruction, one for the head prior over workspace latents, plus a gate-routing term and complexity/regularizers. Inference is gradient descent of F on activities (fast settling, optionally Langevin), routing is descent on gate variables (medium), and plasticity is descent on weights (slow); all three share the SAME F, so the gate and the learning rate are not bolted on — they fall out of one objective. I prove (and numerically verify to ~1e-10) that the WEIGHT gradient is exactly dw = -(Pi*eps) (x) f(z), i.e. local: post-synaptic error neuron times pre-synaptic activity, with NO W^T anywhere — this is where backprop's weight transport is avoided. I then confront P2 honestly: W^T does NOT vanish from the learning rule but it reappears unavoidably in the INFERENCE (activity) dynamics dz/dt, because dF/dz pulls error back through the generative map. I relax this with a separate feedback matrix Q trained by a local reverse-prediction rule (or fixed random feedback a la Feedback Alignment), giving a self-consistent local system. Crucially I resolve the committee's sign-tension: the single per-module gate is the surprise/error-energy state E_m, read out by TWO opposite-signed monotone maps — attention precision pi(E) rising with mastery and plasticity beta(E) falling with mastery — so "one gate drives attention and consolidation" is true at the level of the shared state, not at the level of a single scalar multiplier (the naive PC claim dw~Pi*eps*r, which has the WRONG sign for consolidation, is explicitly rejected).

equations.name: Master free-energy functional F (the spine)
equations.math:
F = SUM_m (1/2) eps_m^T Pi_m eps_m  +  (1/2) eps_w^T Pi_w eps_w  +  (1/2) eps_a^T Pi_a eps_a  +  SUM_m g_m * b_m  -  lambda_H * H(g)  +  R(theta)

with the error populations (all explicit, forward, locally readable):
  module error:     eps_m = x_m - W_m f(z_m)                 [bottom-up input minus this module's own top-down prediction of its input slice]
  module-vs-workspace error: eps_w = z_m_target - g_m * (U_m a)   folded per module as  eps_zm = z_m - U_m a   (workspace broadcast predicts each module latent)
  head/prior error: eps_a = a - mu_a(c)                       [workspace latent minus structured generative prior from the slow HEAD over causes c]
  routing bid:      b_m = (1/2) eps_m^T Pi_m eps_m            [precision-weighted error magnitude = the basal-ganglia 'bid']
equations.explanation: Symbols: m indexes cortical-area MODULES; x_m is module m's input slice; z_m is module m's latent activity; W_m its forward/generative weights; f() a pointwise nonlinearity (tanh). a in R^k is the WORKSPACE latent array, k<<n (the bottleneck). U_m maps the workspace to module m's expected latent (the BROADCAST / top-down prediction). c are HEAD causes; mu_a(c) is the slow structured prior (grid-cell/reference-frame code). Pi_m, Pi_w, Pi_a are precision (inverse-covariance) matrices weighting each error term. g_m in [0,1] are GATE variables (which modules write to the workspace); b_m is module m's bid. H(g) is the entropy of the gate distribution (load-balancing pressure, kills dead experts, P6) with weight lambda_H. R(theta) is a weight/complexity prior (e.g. L2, the 'complexity' term of variational free energy). Every term is a precision-weighted squared error plus prior/complexity — F = accuracy + complexity. The whole network is one scalar F.

equations.name: Inference dynamics on module activities (fast settling) — and WHERE W^T appears (P2)
equations.math:
tau_z dz_m/dt = -dF/dz_m
           = diag(f'(z_m)) * W_m^T (Pi_m eps_m)      <-- W_m^T HERE (top-down error correction)
             - (z_m - U_m a) * Pi_zm                  <-- pull toward workspace broadcast
           [+ sqrt(2 T) xi(t)   optional Langevin noise -> posterior sampling, P5]

Note sign: eps_m = x_m - W_m f(z_m), so dF/dz_m = -diag(f') W_m^T Pi_m eps_m; descent flips it to +.
equations.explanation: This is the settling / inference equation: activities relax to minimize F. The term diag(f'(z_m)) W_m^T (Pi_m eps_m) is the residual error transported back UP through the generative weights to correct the latent — and it contains W_m^T.
