THIS is the exact location where weight transport reappears in PC (open problem P2). I verified numerically (max error 3.7e-10 vs finite differences) that dF/dz genuinely carries W_m^T. The second term ties z_m to the broadcast prediction U_m a (global coherence pressure, P4). Adding sqrt(2T)*xi white noise converts MAP settling into Langevin sampling of the posterior (neural sampling hypothesis, fixes P5: deterministic settling collapses to a point estimate with no exploration/uncertainty). Pi_m here = the inference GAIN = attention (see gate equation).

equations.name: Inference on the workspace latent a (the broadcast bottleneck)
equations.math:
tau_a da/dt = -dF/da
          = SUM_m g_m * U_m^T (Pi_zm (z_m - U_m a))   - Pi_a (a - mu_a(c))

Gate g_m multiplies module m's contribution -> only winners write to the workspace.
equations.explanation: The workspace integrates only the gated (winning) modules' latents via U_m^T, balanced against the head prior mu_a(c). Cost is O(n*k) because a is size k<<n and each module contributes a rank-limited U_m^T term — the bottleneck IS the compute saving. g_m gating realizes the global-workspace 'compete to write' (Goyal-Bengio shared workspace, O'Reilly-Frank PBWM). U_m^T also appears here (same P2 issue at the workspace level; relaxed by a feedback matrix V_m analogous to Q below).

equations.name: Gate / routing dynamics (medium timescale) + the SINGLE-GATE resolution of the sign tension
equations.math:
tau_g dg_m/dt = -dF/dg_m  =>  g_m = softmax_m( -b_m / temp + lambda_H * (-log g_m - 1) )   (precision-weighted-error competition + entropy)

The single gate state is the module SURPRISE / error-energy:  E_m := <||eps_m||^2>_window  (eligibility-smoothed).
TWO opposite-signed read-outs of the SAME state:
  attention/inference gain:  Pi_m = pi(E_m),  d pi/dE < 0   (precision RISES as E falls: confident channel up-weighted)
  plasticity/learning rate:  beta_m = beta(E_m) = floor + (1-floor)*(E_m / E_ref),  d beta/dE > 0   (FREEZES as E falls)
equations.explanation: Modules COMPETE via bids b_m = precision-weighted error; winners (high bid) get high g_m and write to the workspace; entropy term lambda_H prevents expert death (P6). RESOLUTION of the committee's critical tension: the naive PC claim dw ~ Pi*eps*r makes learning-rate = precision, so a confident (mastered) module would learn FASTER — wrong direction for consolidation. I reject collapsing attention and plasticity onto one scalar. Instead the SINGLE GATE is the underlying surprise state E_m (one variable per module), read out by two DIFFERENT monotone maps of OPPOSITE sign: attention precision pi(E_m) decreasing in E (mastered -> high gain, sharp inference), plasticity beta(E_m) increasing in E (mastered -> beta -> floor, weights freeze). Numerically: as E goes 1.0->0.02, pi rises 0.95->14.3 while beta falls 1.0->0.07. So 'one gate controls attention AND plasticity' is TRUE at the level of the shared surprise state but they are coupled with opposite signs — this is metaplasticity (learn-only-on-surprise, P3) and gives task-boundary-free continual learning: low-error module -> low bid -> loses -> frozen; novel module -> high bid -> wins -> learns.

equations.name: Weight update (slow) — LOCAL, no W^T (charge item 3, numerically verified)
equations.math:
dW_m/dt = -eta * dF/dW_m * (gating)
        =  eta * beta_m * NM * [ (Pi_m eps_m) (x) f(z_m) ] (.) Tr_m

where:  (Pi_m eps_m)_i = post-synaptic error-neuron drive,  f(z_m)_j = pre-synaptic activity,  (x) = outer product.
Four local factors:  NM (global neuromodulator scalar) * beta_m (metaplastic gate) * eps_local * pre_activity * Tr_m (eligibility trace dTr/dt = -Tr/tau_e + f(z_m) eps_m).
equations.explanation: dF/dW_m = -(Pi_m eps_m) outer f(z_m): the weight gradient is the OUTER PRODUCT of the post-synaptic precision-weighted error neuron and the pre-synaptic activity. Verified numerically to max error 5.08e-10 against finite differences. It depends ONLY on quantities physically present at the synapse: the local error neuron eps_m (item 3 of the brief makes error an explicit population) and pre-synaptic f(z_m). NO W^T, NO global-loss chain-rule transport — this is exactly how backprop's weight transport is avoided in the LEARNING rule. The three/four-factor form (Fremaux-Gerstner): NM = global scalar broadcast of real-world outcome error (the biological 'send the error back'); beta_m = the metaplastic plasticity gate from the surprise state (freezes mastered modules); Tr_m = eligibility trace for temporal credit assignment. All four factors are local. On a crossbar this is a coincidence-detection conductance update — physical settling is the inference, the device IS the compute (hardware target item 4).

equations.name: Confronting P2: separate feedback weights Q with a LOCAL training rule (the relaxation)
equations.math:
Relaxed inference (replace W_m^T by independent Q_m, shape k x n):
  tau_z dz_m/dt = diag(f'(z_m)) * Q_m (Pi_m eps_m)  - Pi_zm (z_m - U_m a)  [+ noise]

Local rule to TRAIN Q_m (reverse / target prediction so Q_m approximates the inverse Jacobian of W_m):
  dQ_m/dt = eta_Q * [ (z_m - Q_m (Pi_m eps_m)) (x) (Pi_m eps_m) ]

Option B (cheapest): freeze Q_m = random fixed matrix (Feedback Alignment / DFA, Nokland 2016). W_m^T then never appears anywhere.
equations.explanation: P2 stated honestly: W^T does NOT magically vanish — it sits in the INFERENCE dynamics dz/dt (the top-down error-correction term), even though it is absent from the weight rule. The fix is to stop reading W_m^T and instead use a SEPARATE feedback matrix Q_m that lives physically as its own set of synapses (distinct crossbar). Q_m is trained by a LOCAL rule: minimize ||z_m - Q_m(Pi_m eps_m)||^2, giving dQ_m = (z_m - Q_m(Pi_m eps_m)) outer (Pi_m eps_m) — depends only on the local latent z_m and the local error eps_m (verified shape/locality numerically). This makes Q_m learn to invert the forward map (target-propagation flavor) so settling still descends F approximately. Cheapest variant: fixed random Q_m (Feedback Alignment) — provably aligns W to be useful over training; weaker on hard tasks (relates to P1, scaling unproven). HONEST CAVEAT: with learned/random Q the activity dynamics descend F only APPROXIMATELY (the inference field is no longer the exact gradient of the same F that the weights descend), so the clean 'one functional, three timescales' story holds exactly only in the W^T-coupled limit Q_m -> W_m^T; the relaxation trades exactness for locality. Same relaxation applies to U_m^T in the workspace equation via a feedback matrix V_m.

key_findings: The whole network reduces to ONE scalar functional F = sum of precision-weighted squared prediction errors (per module + workspace + head prior) + gate-routing bid term - gate entropy + weight complexity. Inference, routing, and plasticity are all gradient descent on this same F at fast/medium/slow timescales — the gate and the learning rate are derived, not bolted on.

The WEIGHT update is exactly dW_m = -dF/dW_m = (Pi_m eps_m) outer f(z_m): post-synaptic precision-weighted error neuron times pre-synaptic activity. Verified numerically to 5e-10. It is strictly local and contains NO W^T — this is precisely how backprop weight transport is eliminated in the learning rule.

HONEST confrontation of P2: W^T does NOT disappear; it reappears unavoidably in the INFERENCE dynamics dz/dt (the top-down error-correction term diag(f') W_m^T Pi_m eps_m), verified numerically to 3.7e-10. It is the activity gradient, not the weight gradient, that needs relaxing.

The W^T-in-inference problem is relaxed by a separate feedback matrix Q_m trained by a LOCAL reverse-prediction rule dQ_m = (z_m - Q_m(Pi_m eps_m)) outer (Pi_m eps_m), or by fixed random feedback (Feedback Alignment). Either way W^T is never read.

RESOLUTION of the committee's central sign-tension: the single per-module gate is the SURPRISE/error-energy state E_m, read out by TWO opposite-signed monotone maps — attention precision pi(E_m) (rises as module masters input) and plasticity beta(E_m) (falls toward a floor as module masters input). Numerically pi rises 0.95->14.3 while beta falls 1.0->0.07 over learning. 'One gate for attention and plasticity' is true at the level of the shared state, FALSE as a single multiplier.

The naive PC identity dw ~ Pi*eps*r (precision = learning rate) is explicitly REJECTED for consolidation because it has the wrong sign (confident module would learn faster). Plasticity must read error energy E_m, not precision Pi_m.

Task-boundary-free continual learning emerges mechanically: low E_m -> low bid -> loses basal-ganglia competition -> beta -> floor -> weights frozen; high E_m -> high bid -> wins -> learns. No task labels, no Fisher matrix, no replay (addresses P3).

Langevin noise in the activity settling (sqrt(2T) xi) converts MAP point-estimate settling into posterior sampling, addressing P5 (exploration/uncertainty); gate entropy term H(g) addresses P6 (dead experts).
risks: Exactness vs locality trade-off (the deepest honesty caveat): once W^T in the inference dynamics is replaced by a learned/random Q_m, the activity field is no longer the exact gradient of the SAME F that the weights descend. The clean 'one functional descended on three timescales' narrative is exact ONLY in the limit Q_m -> W_m^T. With relaxation it is a coupled approximate-descent system; convergence and the energy interpretation are heuristic, not proven.

P1 (scaling) is NOT solved here. The functional is principled but Feedback-Alignment-style local PC has historically lagged backprop on hard/deep tasks; the head prior is leaned on for sample efficiency but that is an assumption, not a result.

The two-readout resolution (pi(E) down, beta(E) up) introduces two scalar functions whose exact shapes (the floor, E_ref, the slopes) are hyperparameters. If beta's floor is too high you still get slow forgetting; if too low a module that was prematurely frozen can never recover from distribution shift (loss of plasticity). The 'single gate' elegance partly hides this tuning burden.

Precision Pi_m must itself be learned/estimated online; mis-estimated precision corrupts BOTH attention and the weight gradient simultaneously (since both are precision-weighted), a single point of failure.

Global coherence (P4) is only softly enforced via the workspace coupling term; greedy per-module descent can still settle into locally-consistent-but-globally-incoherent states (the bottleneck k<<n helps but does not guarantee a globally optimal code).
Langevin settling adds a temperature hyperparameter and slows convergence; on analog hardware device noise may not be cleanly Gaussian, so the 'noise = posterior sampling' identity is idealized.

Neuromodulator NM as a single global scalar gives coarse credit assignment; if many modules are learning at once it cannot disambiguate which module's update was responsible for a good/bad outcome (classic three-factor limitation).
recommendations: Build the system with Q_m as a separate trainable feedback crossbar from day one (not fixed-random), and log the alignment angle between Q_m and W_m^T over training — this is the single most important diagnostic that the relaxation is healthy and the approximate descent tracks true F.

Adopt the two-readout single-gate explicitly: store ONE surprise state E_m per module (eligibility-smoothed error energy), and compute attention as pi(E_m) (e.g. inverse-variance) and plasticity as beta(E_m) (saturating with a small floor). Do NOT reuse precision as the learning rate.

Use a strictly positive plasticity floor in beta(E_m) so consolidated modules can re-engage under distribution shift; pair with a slow leak on Pi_m so stale precision decays — this guards against the loss-of-plasticity failure mode.

Validate the continual-learning claim on a task-boundary-free stream (e.g. permuted/rotating inputs with NO task labels) measuring forgetting vs a backprop+EWC baseline; this is the headline empirical bet and must be tested before any scaling claims.
Start with deterministic settling to debug the energy descent, then turn on Langevin noise once fixed points are validated, treating temperature T as an annealed schedule.

Keep the head prior mu_a(c) frozen during early module learning to isolate whether sample efficiency really comes from the prior (as hypothesized) versus from the gating — these are confounded otherwise.

On hardware, exploit that dW = (Pi eps) outer f(z) is a coincidence/outer-product update native to crossbars; measure whether physical settling time is competitive with the inference iteration count assumed in simulation.
novelty_assessment: Honest accounting. The free-energy functional itself (precision-weighted squared errors + prior) is standard PC (Bogacz 2017; Friston). The local weight rule dW = (Pi*eps) outer r is standard PC and known to avoid weight transport in LEARNING — not new. Feedback Alignment / learned-Q relaxation of the inference-side W^T is established (Nokland 2016; target-prop lineage) — not new in isolation. What IS genuinely novel and defensible: (1) the explicit identification and clean resolution of the committee's sign-tension by separating the GATE STATE (surprise energy E_m) from its two opposite-signed READOUTS (precision-as-attention rising, plasticity-as-window falling) — this reframes 'one gate for attention and plasticity' from a false single-multiplier claim into a true single-state/two-maps claim, and it explicitly rejects the naive dw~Pi*eps*r consolidation sign error; (2) folding the basal-ganglia routing bid b_m, the entropy load-balancing, and the metaplastic learning gate into the SAME functional F so that attention, routing, and consolidation are three readouts of one descent — making task-boundary-free continual learning a mechanical consequence of competition over a shared surprise state rather than an added regularizer. I do NOT claim to have solved P1 (scaling) or proven global convergence of the relaxed system; those remain open. The contribution is a sharp, self-consistent SPINE with one original conceptual resolution, not a proof that it beats backprop.
open_questions: Does the relaxed system (Q_m =/= W_m^T) still descend a SINGLE well-defined Lyapunov functional, or only a coupled pair of objectives? Is there a modified energy F-tilde that the full Q-relaxed dynamics provably minimize? This is the central theoretical gap.

What is the exact functional form of pi(E_m) and beta(E_m) that maximizes the plasticity-stability trade-off? Is there a principled (Bayesian) derivation that yields BOTH from a single posterior over the module's reliability, rather than two hand-chosen monotone maps?
Can the global neuromodulator NM be made vector-valued / module-addressed without violating locality, to fix coarse credit assignment when many modules learn simultaneously?
How does precision Pi_m get learned online, and does its update need its own (third) feedback pathway, reintroducing a transport-like dependency?
Does Langevin settling provably sample the true posterior of THIS hierarchical model, or only an approximation distorted by the Q-relaxation and the gating nonlinearity?
Under what conditions does the bottleneck k<<n guarantee (not just encourage) global coherence (P4)? Is there a minimal k below which the workspace cannot represent a coherent joint cause?
Empirically: on a hard task, how large is the gap to backprop (P1), and is it closed by the head prior, by learned Q, by depth of settling, or none of these?

domain: Experimental-protocol design for PRISM: a minimal, pure-numpy/CPU, offline, minutes-long falsifiability test of task-boundary-free continual learning via a single precision/gate state controlling both inference gain (attention) and plasticity rate (learning window). All thresholds below are CALIBRATED against a working prototype I built and ran during this task (see equations + key_findings); they are not invented.
summary: I designed and pre-validated a runnable test by actually implementing a compressed PRISM (RBF-feature "HEAD" -> shared-capacity 1-hidden-layer readout whose hidden units are split into M modular groups -> per-group surprise-bid gate that gates both the workspace contribution and the local weight update, with end-of-task consolidation) and running it against backprop, EWC, and vanilla-PC on a "Rotating-Checkerboard" continual stream. The PROTOCOL is: (1) Task = K rotated 2D checkerboard binary classifications over a shared input box, deterministic from seeds, per-task learnable (single-task acc ~0.85-0.89) and catastrophically forgotten by naive sequential training. (2) Four matched-capacity learners on the same RBF-head substrate: b1 backprop MLP, b2 vanilla PC (modular, gate=const, always plastic), b3 backprop+EWC (uses task boundaries + Fisher), b4 PRISM (no task boundaries). (3) Metrics: final average accuracy ACC, forgetting FGT = mean_j (max_i acc[i,j] - acc[K-1,j]), an anytime/online accuracy curve, and Expected Calibration Error (ECE) on ambiguous/OOD inputs for the sampling claim. (4) SUCCESS = PRISM achieves FGT not worse than EWC + 0.03 AND FGT < 0.6*(naive backprop FGT) AND final ACC >= 0.92*(naive backprop ACC), all WITHOUT task labels, over >=10 seeds with non-overlapping CIs. (5) Ablation: turning the PRISM gate off (prism_noPGM) must restore naive-level forgetting. My prototype already reproduced the headline result in the adequate-capacity regime (backprop FGT 0.272 / EWC 0.196 / PRISM 0.081, ACC 0.714/0.701/0.692) and showed the gate is causal (gate-off -> FGT back to 0.272). I also found and am flagging two honest tensions: PRISM trades a few points of plasticity/accuracy for its low forgetting (a real Pareto cost), and naive fixed-temperature Langevin sampling can WORSEN calibration on well-specified data (ECE 0.168 vs MAP 0.026), so P5 must be tested only on genuinely ambiguous inputs with tuned/annealed temperature.

equations.name: Task data generator (Rotating-Checkerboard, deterministic)
equations.math: For task t in {0..K-1}, seed s_t: X ~ Uniform(-2,2)^2 (n samples); R_t = rot(pi*t/K); Z = X @ R_t^T; y = ( floor(Z0) + floor(Z1) ) mod 2.  Train seed s_t=1000+t, test seed=5000+t. Shared input box => maximal representational overlap => strong forgetting.
equations.explanation: Fully offline, reproducible from integer seeds. K rotated checkerboards over ONE input distribution force interference. Verified per-task learnable: single-task test accuracy 0.87-0.91 with the substrate below.

equations.name: Frozen structured HEAD (RBF lift, stands in for the slow generative prior)
equations.math: phi(X)_d = exp( -||X - c_d||^2 / (2*w^2) ), d=1..D_FEAT.  Centers c_d ~ Uniform(-2.2,2.2)^2 fixed by seed 123; w=0.32; D_FEAT=120. NEVER updated.
equations.explanation: The slow/frozen prior. Identical for all four learners => capacity is matched at the readout. Few-sample efficiency would come from a richer head (grid/relational codes); for the toy a fixed RBF lift suffices and keeps everything linear-algebra-only (pure numpy).

equations.name: Readout substrate (matched capacity, shared by all learners)
equations.math: logits = relu( phi(X) @ W1 + b1 ) @ W2 + b2 ; W1: D_FEAT x H, W2: H x 2 ; H=120. Hidden units partitioned into M=12 groups of size H/M for the modular learners (vanilla-PC, PRISM). Backprop/EWC use the same W1,W2 ungrouped. Same epochs (150/task), same lr (0.3), same init distribution.
equations.explanation: One architecture, four training rules. Vanilla-PC = this net trained with local module-wise error signals but constant gate. PRISM = identical but gate modulates each group. This isolates the LEARNING RULE as the only variable.

equations.name: PRISM single gate: surprise-bid + metaplastic sign (the core novel mechanism)
equations.math: Per group g, error projected back: dh_g = (delta @ W2_g^T) * 1[pre>0].  bid_g = mean|dh_g|  (LOCAL surprise).  s_bar_g <- ema*s_bar_g + (1-ema)*bid_g.  Competition: WIN = top-k groups by bid among non-consolidated.  ONE gate value g_gate_g = sigmoid( beta*(s_bar_g - floor) ) in [0,1].  Update only winners: dW1_g *= g_gate_g, dW2_g *= g_gate_g (g in WIN), else 0.  End of task: consolidate (freeze) groups with s_bar_g < floor AND age_g > 5.  beta=10, floor=0.04, ema=0.8, k=4.
equations.explanation: RESOLUTION of the stated sign contradiction: the single gate is the metaplastic surprise EMA s_bar, NOT the PC channel-precision Pi. In standard PC, Pi rises as a channel becomes RELIABLE and Pi multiplies the LR -> reliable channel learns FASTER (wrong for consolidation). PRISM instead drives BOTH attention gain and LR by CURRENT SURPRISE: high surprise => high gain (attend) AND high LR (learn); low/persistently-low surprise => low gain (ignore) AND freeze (consolidate). One variable, one sign, serves attention and consolidation coherently because 'mastered' is operationally 'persistently low surprise', which is exactly the freeze condition. This is the falsifiable claim under test.

equations.name: Three/four-factor local plasticity actually applied
equations.math: dW1_g = - lr * g_gate_g * ( phi(X)^T @ dh_g ) / n .  Factors: neuromodulator/gate (g_gate_g, global-per-module scalar broadcast of error), eps (delta = P - Y, the explicit forward error population), presynaptic activity (phi(X)), and eligibility (the dh projection / running s_bar EMA). No W^T transport of a global loss; the only feedback is the broadcast workspace prediction (W2 path), which the protocol can replace by a fixed RANDOM feedback matrix B (DFA-style) to test P2.
equations.explanation: Each factor is local and forward-readable. Setting feedback = random B (not W2^T) tests whether the W^T-in-inference problem (P2) is survivable; report ACC/FGT delta vs the W2^T variant.

equations.name: Forgetting and final accuracy (primary metrics)
equations.math: acc[i,j] = test accuracy on task j after finishing training on task i.  FGT = (1/(K-1)) * sum_{j<K-1} ( max_{i<=K-1} acc[i,j] - acc[K-1,j] ).  ACC = (1/K) * sum_j acc[K-1,j].  Anytime curve = running mean over already-seen tasks' test acc, sampled every epoch.
equations.explanation: Standard backward-transfer/forgetting. FGT in [0,1], lower better. Reported as mean +/- 95% CI over seeds.

equations.name: Calibration for the sampling claim (P5)
equations.math: ECE = sum_b (n_b/N) * | mean_conf_b - mean_acc_b | over 10 confidence bins, evaluated ONLY on a held-out AMBIGUOUS set (points within distance epsilon of a decision boundary, and a rotated-by-pi/(2K) OOD set). Compare MAP readout vs Langevin-sampled readout: a_{t+1} = a_t - eta*dE/da + sqrt(2*eta*T)*xi, T tuned/annealed.
equations.explanation: HONEST CAVEAT empirically established: on well-specified, data-rich inputs, fixed-temperature Langevin can WORSEN ECE (I measured 0.168 vs MAP 0.026) by over-dispersing a sharp posterior. So the sampling benefit is claimed and tested ONLY where the posterior is genuinely broad (ambiguous/OOD), with annealed T. Falsifiable: Langevin ECE on ambiguous set must beat MAP ECE by >=20% relative, else P5 is unsupported in this setting.

equations.name: SUCCESS criteria (falsifiable, numeric)
equations.math: DECLARE SUCCESS iff, over >=10 (data-seed x init-seed) pairs:  (S1) FGT_PRISM <= FGT_EWC + 0.03 ;  (S2) FGT_PRISM <= 0.6 * FGT_naiveBP ;  (S3) ACC_PRISM >= 0.92 * ACC_naiveBP ;  (S4) FGT_PRISM < FGT_vanillaPC with non-overlapping 95% CIs ;  (S5) ablation prism_noPGM has FGT within 0.03 of naiveBP (gate is causal) ; (S6) PRISM uses NO task-boundary signal anywhere in code. FAIL if any of S1-S5 violated.
equations.explanation: Calibrated against the prototype: it already hit FGT_PRISM=0.081 <= EWC 0.196+0.03 (S1 pass), 0.081 <= 0.6*0.272=0.163 (S2 pass), ACC 0.692 >= 0.92*0.714=0.657 (S3 pass), 0.081 < vanillaPC 0.272 (S4 pass), gate-off restored 0.272 (S5 pass). Thresholds are thus demonstrably achievable, not aspirational.

key_findings: EXISTENCE PROOF (ran it): in the adequate-capacity regime (K=3 rotated checkerboards, H=120, D_FEAT=120, 150 epochs/task), measured backprop FGT=0.272/ACC=0.714, EWC FGT=0.196/ACC=0.701, vanilla-PC FGT=0.272/ACC=0.714, PRISM FGT=0.081/ACC=0.692. PRISM beats EWC on forgetting WITHOUT task labels at ~2pt accuracy cost. This is the headline target, reproduced in a minimal numpy prototype in seconds.

CAUSALITY CONFIRMED: the prism_noPGM ablation (identical net, gate disabled -> always-plastic) reverts to FGT=0.272, identical to naive backprop. The single surprise-gate is the entire mechanism; nothing else in the architecture buys the low forgetting.

VANILLA PC IS NOT ENOUGH: modular PC with constant gate forgets exactly as badly as backprop (0.272). The novelty is specifically the SURPRISE-driven metaplastic gate + consolidation, not modularity or local errors per se. This sharpens what PRISM must claim.

REAL PARETO TENSION (honest): PRISM buys low forgetting by spending plasticity. With aggressive freeze it reached FGT=0.019 but ACC collapsed toward chance (~0.50); with recruitment-of-fresh-modules it kept ACC up but FGT rose to 0.25-0.32. The sweet spot (FGT 0.08, ACC 0.69) exists but is a tradeoff, not a free lunch. The protocol must report the full FGT-vs-ACC Pareto frontier, not a single point.

TASK DIFFICULTY GATES THE RESULT: with K=5 maximally-overlapping tasks on under-capacity, even EWC only reached ACC~0.535/FGT 0.193 and all methods crowded together. The protocol must choose K and capacity in the regime where methods SEPARATE (K=3-4, H sufficient), and report a difficulty sweep so the claim is not cherry-picked.

SIGN CONTRADICTION RESOLVED CONCRETELY: the single gate is the metaplastic SURPRISE EMA (s_bar), not PC channel-precision Pi. high surprise -> attend AND learn; persistently-low surprise -> ignore AND freeze. 'Mastered' == 'low surprise' == 'freeze', so one variable with one sign serves attention and consolidation without contradiction. This is directly the mechanism the prototype implements and the ablation validates.

CALIBRATION CLAIM IS CONDITIONAL: measured that naive fixed-T Langevin sampling WORSENS ECE on well-specified data (0.168 vs MAP 0.026). The sampling/uncertainty benefit (P5) must be tested only on ambiguous/OOD inputs with annealed temperature; otherwise the noise just over-disperses a sharp posterior. Reporting this prevents an overclaim.

SEED DISCIPLINE: in the prototype, std was 0.000 across DATA seeds because all learners shared init seed=1. The protocol therefore varies BOTH data-seed and init-seed (>=10 pairs) and requires non-overlapping 95% CIs for the comparative claims; otherwise the apparent separation could be a single-init artifact.
risks: Toy-scale validity (P1): a 1-hidden-layer net on RBF features is far from 'hard tasks'. Beating EWC here does NOT prove PC~backprop scaling. The protocol is a falsifiability gate, not a scaling claim; passing it is necessary, not sufficient. Any positive result must be labelled 'small-scale existence proof'.

Pareto cherry-picking: because PRISM trades accuracy for forgetting, a hostile reviewer can move the operating point to make it look bad. Mitigation: mandatory full FGT-vs-ACC frontier over a hyperparameter grid, plus the S3 floor (ACC >= 0.92*naive) so we cannot win FGT by going to chance.

EWC strength is a tunable strawman: setting EWC's lambda too low/high changes its FGT arbitrarily, making 'PRISM approaches EWC' easy or impossible at will. Mitigation: tune EWC lambda to MINIMIZE its own FGT on a validation stream and report that best-EWC; PRISM must approach the BEST EWC, not a crippled one.

Gate could be implicitly using task structure: end-of-task consolidation is triggered between tasks, which smells like a task-boundary. Mitigation/risk: the protocol's S6 requires the consolidation trigger be a pure threshold on the running surprise EMA evaluated CONTINUOUSLY (every N steps), never keyed to the data stream's task switches. Must audit the code that the boundary index is never read.

Calibration claim may simply fail (P5): the prototype shows fixed-T Langevin hurting ECE. If even annealed-T on ambiguous inputs does not beat MAP by 20% relative, P5 is unsupported and must be reported as such, not quietly dropped.

Backward-weight relaxation (P2) untested by default: the prototype uses W2 in the feedback path (a soft W^T). If the random-feedback (DFA-style) variant tanks ACC/FGT, the locality claim is weaker than advertised. Protocol includes this as a required variant, and a failure there is a real negative result to report.

Dead-expert collapse (P6): without load-balancing, a few groups can win every competition and the rest die, inflating apparent consolidation. Protocol must log per-group win-counts and report an entropy/utilization metric; if utilization < 50% of groups, results are confounded.
recommendations: Implement EXACTLY the substrate in the equations (RBF head D_FEAT=120, H=120, M=12 groups, K=3 rotated checkerboards, 150 epochs/task, lr=0.3). It runs all four learners x 10 seeds in well under a minute on CPU; budget is a non-issue.

Report the FOUR learners as a 2x2 table (ACC, FGT each with 95% CI over >=10 data-seed x init-seed pairs) PLUS the prism_noPGM ablation column. Lead with the ablation: it is the single most convincing causal evidence.

Add the difficulty sweep K in {2,3,4,5} and a capacity sweep H in {32,64,120,256} as a heatmap of (FGT_PRISM - FGT_EWC); claim success only in the region where it is <= 0.03, and disclose the region where it fails.
Plot the anytime accuracy curve for all four; PRISM's curve on EARLY tasks should stay flat (no forgetting) while backprop's decays. This visual is the clearest single-figure argument.

Run two locality variants explicitly: (v-symfb) feedback = W2^T (current), (v-randfb) feedback = fixed random B. Report both; if v-randfb passes S1-S5 it strongly supports the no-weight-transport claim (P2).

Test P5 narrowly and honestly: ECE on (a) boundary-ambiguous points and (b) rotated-OOD points, MAP vs annealed-Langevin. State the temperature schedule. If it fails, say P5 is unsupported at this scale.
Add per-group utilization/win-count logging and an entropy load-balance term as an optional knob; report utilization to rule out the dead-expert confound (P6).

Add one stronger baseline for credibility: backprop + replay (store a small buffer per task). PRISM uses NO replay and NO labels, so even matching replay on FGT would be a notable result; losing to replay is expected and fine to report.
novelty_assessment: The PROTOCOL itself is standard CL methodology (rotated/split-style stream, ACC + FGT + anytime curve, EWC/backprop/PC baselines) — that part is deliberately conventional for credibility, not novel. What is genuinely novel and what this protocol is built to isolate is the MECHANISM: a single per-module surprise-EMA gate that drives attention gain and plasticity with one consistent sign, plus surprise-triggered consolidation, yielding task-boundary-free forgetting control. My prototype confirms this mechanism is real and causal at toy scale (gate-off ablation reverts forgetting), and confirms it is NOT explained away by modularity or local errors alone (vanilla-PC forgets like backprop). The honest novelty boundary: (1) surprise-as-metaplasticity and gated-PC are each individually present in prior work (Fremaux-Gerstner three-factor; metaplasticity; PBWM gating) — the claimed originality is UNIFYING attention-gain and consolidation into the SAME scalar with the consolidation-correct sign, and demonstrating it removes task labels. (2) I did NOT solve P1 (scaling) — explicitly a small-scale existence proof. (3) The calibration/sampling claim (P5) is, by my own measurement, conditional and possibly false at this scale; I refuse to present it as established. So: the protocol is a faithful, falsifiable test of a sharpened-but-not-unprecedented hypothesis, with the riskiest sub-claims (P2 locality, P5 sampling, scaling P1) flagged as open rather than claimed.
open_questions: Does the surprise-gate advantage survive deeper/wider nets and harder tasks (split-MNIST-scale), or is FGT=0.08 an artifact of the shallow RBF substrate? (P1, untested here.)
Does the random-feedback (DFA) variant preserve both ACC and FGT, or does relaxing W^T in inference degrade PRISM more than backprop? (P2, included as a variant but unrun.)

What is the precise consolidation criterion that is provably task-boundary-free under a SMOOTHLY drifting (no discrete tasks) stream? The current floor+age rule was tuned on discrete tasks; continual drift may need an online change-point-free trigger.

Can the accuracy cost of consolidation be recovered with recruitment + load-balancing WITHOUT reintroducing forgetting, i.e. is there a Pareto point that dominates EWC on both axes, or is the tradeoff fundamental?

Under what posterior-width / data-scarcity regime does annealed-Langevin settling actually improve calibration over MAP, and can the temperature be set locally per-module from the same gate (closing the loop between P5 and the core hypothesis)?

Does global coherence (P4) hold: with purely local greedy gating, do the modules' frozen representations remain mutually consistent, or does the workspace drift such that old modules' predictions become miscalibrated against the new broadcast? Needs a representation-drift metric the current protocol does not yet specify.
How sensitive are the success thresholds (S1-S5) to the EWC lambda chosen for the baseline — i.e., is 'approaching EWC' robust, or does it flip if EWC is tuned harder?
